question: The categories of admixtures are concrete, mortar and ________:
keyMemory: Sa true admixture concept, ang mahalaga ay integral siya sa mix, hindi surface-applied.
explanation:
Best answer: b. integral application finishes
Ulitin natin: integral application finishes.
Ito, aaminin ko: medyo sablay ang wording ng item. Hindi siya malinis pakinggan technically.
Pero kailangan pa rin natin piliin ang best answer among the choices.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
Ang word na admixture usually means material mixed into the concrete or mortar — in other words, integral siya, hindi surface-applied.
Kaya sa options, ang pinaka-malapit at pinaka-defensible ay integral application finishes.
Bakit hindi surface application finishes?
Kasi ang surface-applied material is generally not an admixture in the strict sense. Admixture is incorporated in the mix.
Bakit hindi all of the above?
Kung strict technical reading ang gamit natin, hindi puwedeng isama ang surface application finishes as admixtures. Kaya mahina si all of the above.
Bakit hindi none?
May choice na mas malapit sa tamang concept, which is integral.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Ano ang essence ng admixture?
Hinahalo sa loob ng mix
2.	Alin ang mas tugma roon — surface or integral?
Integral
3.	Kaya kahit awkward ang wording, doon ka pupunta
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay ang pang-akit ng all of the above kapag magulo ang question.
Pero huwag agad kukuha ng all of the above unless talagang malinaw na lahat ay tama.
Important note
Kung mas technical ang usapan, sasabihin natin na poorly worded ang item. Pero for reviewer-answering, integral application finishes ang pinaka-best fit.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa true admixture concept, ang mahalaga ay integral siya sa mix, hindi surface-applied.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: What is the correct term used for plasticizing agent?
keyMemory: Plasticizing agent = water-reducing agent.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. water-reducing agents
Ulitin natin: water-reducing agents.
Ito, standard terminology ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang plasticizer ay admixture na nagpapaganda ng workability ng concrete or mortar without requiring more water, or helps reduce the water needed.
Kaya ang tamang term ay water-reducing agent.
Bakit mali ang iba?
•	water-proofing agents – ibang function ito
•	water-integrating agents – hindi standard term
•	water-borne agents – hindi ito ang term sa admixture classification
Elimination method
1.	Plasticizer improves workability
2.	How? By reducing water demand
3.	Therefore: water-reducing agent
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay pagkapit sa salitang may “water” lang.
Pero ang kilalang technical pair ay:
plasticizer = water-reducer
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Plasticizing agent = water-reducing agent.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: An example of substances added to cement, mortars, concrete for improving or imparting particular properties are called:
keyMemory: Anything added to concrete or mortar to modify properties is generally called admixture.
explanation:
Correct answer: Admixtures
Ulitin natin: Admixtures.
Wala nang visible choices dito sa upload, pero based sa stem alone, ang tamang technical term ay admixtures.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang admixtures ay substances added to:
•	cement mixes
•	mortar
•	concrete
para baguhin o pagandahin ang specific properties tulad ng:
•	workability
•	setting time
•	water resistance
•	color
•	durability
Elimination method kahit walang choices
Kapag ang tanong ay:
•	“substances added”
•	“to improve or impart properties”
ang term na hahanapin mo ay admixtures.
Common trap
Minsan nalilito ito sa additives sa casual use.
Pero sa standard construction materials language, ang term ay admixtures.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Anything added to concrete or mortar to modify properties is generally called admixture.

question: What brand name is not a hardener?
keyMemory: Sa choices na ito, ang hindi hardener na expected reviewer answer ay CELITE.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. CELITE
Ulitin natin: CELITE.
Ito, reviewer-memory item ito, medyo trade-name based. Hindi ito yung tipong pure code question, so ang strategy dito ay brand association at odd-one-out elimination.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa choices, ang CELITE ang pinaka-hindi tugma bilang concrete hardener brand.
Ang ibang names ay tunog hardener/product trade names used in floor or concrete surface treatment context.
Ang Celite is more commonly associated with siliceous/filter/diatomaceous material identity, hindi iyong typical hardener brand na gusto ng reviewer.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	FLORHAND – tunog at gamit nito ay aligned sa floor/concrete hardener type products
•	ARITROC – same, reviewer-type trade name association
•	METALICRON – very suggestive pa nga ng metallic hardener/floor hardener concept
•	CELITE – ito ang outlier
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Tanong ba ay product chemistry? Hindi.
2.	Tanong ba ay brand familiarity / odd-one-out? Oo.
3.	Alin sa apat ang pinaka-hindi tunog hardener o floor treatment product?
CELITE
4.	Kaya iyon ang iiwan mo as answer.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay kung hindi ka familiar sa trade names, mapapapili ka lang sa random.
Ang pinakamagandang gawin sa board kapag ganito ay:
•	hanapin ang may pinakahinang association sa mismong use
•	huwag magpa-impress sa tunog ng pangalan
Important note
Ito ay reviewer-memory item, hindi high-code item. Kaya ang best move ay tandaan siya as an exception question.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa choices na ito, ang hindi hardener na expected reviewer answer ay CELITE.

question: Lumber used for forms of concrete is:
keyMemory: Para sa rough temporary concrete formwork lumber, ang reviewer answer ay green lumber.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. green lumber
Ulitin natin: green lumber.
Ito, practical construction question ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa conventional formwork use, green lumber ang karaniwang sagot dahil:
•	mas practical gamitin sa temporary works
•	hindi ito premium-finish lumber
•	kapag may moisture, hindi agad ganoon ka-brittle compared sa overly dried wood
•	mas swak sa rough temporary use tulad ng forms
Sa reviewer logic, form lumber is not finish lumber, not the kind you reserve for permanent or finish-grade carpentry.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. kiln-dried lumber – mas bagay ito sa permanent carpentry/interior woodwork kung saan kailangan controlled moisture content
•	c. finished lumber – definitely not for rough temporary concrete forms
•	d. partially seasoned lumber – puwedeng tunog practical, pero hindi ito ang classic reviewer answer
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Ang forms ba ay temporary rough-use work o finish work?
Temporary rough-use.
2.	Kaya tanggal agad ang finished lumber
3.	Kiln-dried? mas premium at mas pang-finish/permanent woodwork
4.	Natitira ang pinakapractical temporary formwork answer: green lumber
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay isipin na mas “better quality” ang tamang sagot.
Sa board exam, tandaan mo:
Hindi dahil mas maganda o mas mahal ang material, iyon na ang tamang sagot.
Dapat sakto sa actual use.
Philippine practice
Sa actual practice ngayon, maraming formworks na gumagamit ng plywood, steel forms, at iba’t ibang proprietary systems. Pero sa old-school form-lumber reviewer logic, green lumber ang standard answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Para sa rough temporary concrete formwork lumber, ang reviewer answer ay green lumber.

question: What is the minimum thickness of plywood forms used for columns or beams?
keyMemory: Sa reviewer/formwork memory item, minimum plywood thickness for columns or beams = 1/2 inch.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. 1/2 inch
Ulitin natin: 1/2 inch.
Ito, practical formwork memory item ito. At sa choices, ang pinaka-reasonable minimum ay 1/2 inch, which is about 12 mm.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Para sa columns or beams, ang formwork ay kailangang may sapat na rigidity para hindi basta mag-warp o mag-deform sa pressure ng fresh concrete.
Ang 1/2-inch plywood ang pinaka-common minimum practical answer sa ganitong reviewer question.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. 6 mm – masyadong manipis para sa normal column/beam form use
•	c. 1/4 inch – mas lalong manipis, madaling mag-deflect
•	d. 16 mm – puwedeng gamitin sa ibang mas demanding situations, pero hindi ito ang minimum na tinatanong
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Ang tanong ay minimum, hindi typical heavy-duty size
2.	6 mm at 1/4 inch — obvious na manipis masyado
3.	Between 1/2 inch and 16 mm, alin ang mas logical na minimum?
1/2 inch
4.	16 mm mas mabigat na choice na iyon, hindi na minimum
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay 16 mm, kasi mukhang mas “structurally safe.”
Pero ulitin ko: ang tanong ay minimum, hindi best possible.
Important note
Sa actual formwork design, thickness depends on:
•	stud/waler spacing
•	concrete pressure
•	reuse condition
•	proprietary form system
Pero sa reviewer framing, 1/2 inch ang best answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer/formwork memory item, minimum plywood thickness for columns or beams = 1/2 inch.

visualSourceRefs.label: USDA Forest Products Laboratory — Wood Handbook

question: Forms for concrete require a coating to prevent concrete from adhering to the wood such as:
keyMemory: Sa reviewer logic, ang coating para hindi dumikit ang concrete sa wood form ay crude oil — pero sa modern practice, mas proper ang dedicated release agent.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. crude oil
Ulitin natin: crude oil.
Ito, reviewer answer ito, though gusto kong linawin ang practical nuance.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa old reviewer logic, ang hinahanap dito ay iyong ginagamit bilang release coating para hindi dumikit ang concrete sa wood form. Sa choices, ang pinaka-fit na sagot ay crude oil.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. lacquer paint – finish coating ito, hindi form-release agent
•	c. latex paint – wall paint type use, hindi para sa release function
•	d. weatherproofing paint – protective paint ito, hindi form-release answer
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Ang kailangan ba ay paint finish o release coat?
Release coat.
2.	Kaya tanggal agad ang mga normal paint options
3.	Ang natitirang pinaka-fit sa reviewer choices ay crude oil
Common trap
May examinee na mapapaisip na “parang hindi maganda gamitin ang crude oil.”
At tama ka — sa modern practice, mas okay ang proper form oil o proprietary release agent kaysa crude oil.
Pero dahil choices-based reviewer item ito, crude oil ang expected answer.
Philippine practice note
Sa actual good practice today, mas tama sabihin na form release agents or form oil ang ginagamit, hindi basta crude oil.
Pero sa reviewer/ALE old-school item, crude oil ang best-fit answer sa choices.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer logic, ang coating para hindi dumikit ang concrete sa wood form ay crude oil — pero sa modern practice, mas proper ang dedicated release agent.

question: This is the mixture of sand and cement deposited under high pneumatic pressure with a machine and is used for encasing structural steel and is ideal for swimming pool construction:
keyMemory: Gunite = sand + cement sprayed under pressure, classic for swimming pools and similar work.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Gunite
Ulitin natin: Gunite.
Ito, very classic materials question ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang gunite ay mixture ng cement and sand na ini-spray under high pneumatic pressure.
Common uses:
•	swimming pools
•	repair works
•	encasing steel
•	thin shell or sprayed concrete applications
Exactly tugma sa definition.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Porete – lightweight/processed concrete type ito, hindi pneumatically applied sand-cement mix
•	c. Aerocrete – lightweight expanded structural concrete ito
•	d. Perlite – lightweight insulating aggregate/material ito, hindi sprayed concrete mix
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Hanapin ang main clue: under high pneumatic pressure
2.	Another clue: sand and cement
3.	Another clue: swimming pool construction
4.	Kapag pinagsama mo iyan, Gunite agad ang lalabas
Common trap
Walang masyadong trap dito kung kabisado mo ang word na pneumatic application.
Kapag spray-applied concrete/sand-cement ang tanong, gunite ang strong answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Gunite = sand + cement sprayed under pressure, classic for swimming pools and similar work.

question: One type of processed concrete is:
keyMemory: Kapag magulo ang reviewer item, piliin mo ang pinaka-recognized at pinaka-supported na term — dito, Aerocrete.
explanation:
Best answer: b. Aerocrete
Ulitin natin: Aerocrete.
Aaminin ko dito: medyo awkward ang item, kasi may choices na tunog trade names at may ilan ding mukhang mali o questionable spelling. Pero among the choices, ang pinaka-best answer ay Aerocrete.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
Sa page mismo, may explanatory note sa ibaba tungkol sa Aerocrete, at malinaw na isa siya sa mga kinikilalang processed concrete types sa reviewer context.
Bakit hindi ang iba?
•	a. Cellucrete – may tunog na possible product/material name, pero hindi ito ang mas strongly supported ng page notes
•	c. Gunicrete – mukhang derivative/misspelled or less standard reviewer term compared with Gunite
•	d. Perrocrete – mukhang halatang distractor
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Kapag broad at medyo magulo ang item, hanapin mo ang pinaka-clearly recognized term
2.	Tignan mo rin ang page notes — alin ang mismong iniexplain?
Aerocrete
3.	Kaya siya ang strongest answer
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay pagpilit gawing sobrang technical ang item kahit reviewer-memory lang siya.
Sa ganitong tanong, minsan ang best method ay:
•	hanapin ang pinaka-legit at pinaka-supported na term sa page
Important note
Hindi ito kasing linis ng ibang items. So ang safe ALE move ay piliin ang best-supported answer among choices, which is Aerocrete.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag magulo ang reviewer item, piliin mo ang pinaka-recognized at pinaka-supported na term — dito, Aerocrete.

visualSourceRefs.label: Portland Cement Association — Applications of Cement

question: This is processed concrete added with lightweight aggregate:
keyMemory: Kapag ang tanong ay naghahanap ng processed concrete, huwag kang sasagot ng aggregate name lang kung may mas buo at mas tamang concrete-type answer.
explanation:
Best answer: d. Aerocrete
Ulitin natin: Aerocrete.
Ito rin, medyo hindi super linis ang wording, kaya kailangan nating hanapin ang best-fit answer.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
Sa choices:
•	Gunite = spray-applied sand-cement mix, not lightweight aggregate-type processed concrete
•	Haydite = lightweight aggregate itself, not really the processed concrete name
•	Porete = more associated with chemical foam / porous lightweight product
•	Aerocrete = lightweight expanded structural concrete, kaya siya ang best-fit answer sa phrase na processed concrete with lightweight character
So kung ang hanap ay processed concrete at hindi lang mismong aggregate, Aerocrete ang pinaka-buo at pinaka-defensible na sagot.
Bakit hindi Haydite?
Ito ang common trap.
Oo, ang Haydite ay lightweight aggregate. Pero ang tanong ay processed concrete, hindi lang “anong lightweight aggregate.”
Kaya hindi sapat si Haydite bilang sagot.
Bakit hindi Porete?
Malapit sana, kasi lightweight processed concrete din ang dating niya.
Pero sa wording na “added with lightweight aggregate,” mas tumatama ang Aerocrete as the best available processed-concrete answer among choices.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Tanong ba ay aggregate lang o processed concrete?
Processed concrete.
2.	Kaya tanggal si Haydite kasi aggregate name lang siya
3.	Tanggal si Gunite kasi pneumatic mix iyon
4.	Between Porete and Aerocrete, alin ang mas kilalang lightweight processed concrete answer sa page notes?
Aerocrete
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito si Haydite dahil nakita mo ang phrase na lightweight aggregate.
Pero huwag mong kalimutan ang mas mahalagang word:
processed concrete.
Important note
Poorly worded ang item, oo. Pero sa best-fit reviewer answering, Aerocrete ang pinakamalakas na choice.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang tanong ay naghahanap ng processed concrete, huwag kang sasagot ng aggregate name lang kung may mas buo at mas tamang concrete-type answer.

visualSourceRefs.label: Portland Cement Association — Applications of Cement

question: A built-up construction or combination of building materials as clay, concrete, or stone set in mortar or plain concrete is known as:
keyMemory: Kapag ang clue ay units bonded with mortar like clay, stone, or concrete units, ang sagot ay masonry.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Masonry
Ulitin natin: Masonry.
Ito, very direct definition item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang masonry ay built-up construction using units like:
•	stone
•	brick
•	concrete block
•	clay products
na pinagbubuklod gamit ang mortar o minsan plain concrete, depende sa system.
So eksaktong tugma ang definition sa masonry.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Wall – masyadong general. Ang wall ay maaaring iba’t iba ang construction type.
•	c. Riprap – stone lining ito for erosion control, hindi built-up wall construction term
•	d. Fence – enclosure element ito, hindi material/construction-system definition
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Tanong ba ay construction system/material method? Oo.
2.	Hanapin ang broad technical term para sa built-up units in mortar
3.	Sagot: masonry
4.	Ang wall at fence ay functional objects, hindi exact construction category
5.	Ang riprap ay erosion-control stone work
Common trap
Minsan pipiliin ng examinee ang wall dahil built-up construction nga naman iyon.
Pero ang tanong ay hindi “anong object ito?”
Ang tanong ay anong tawag sa ganitong uri ng construction.
At iyon ay masonry.
Philippine context
Very important ito sa ALE dahil malaking parte ng local construction practice ang CHB walls, brick, stone, mortar-bonded units — lahat iyan pumapasok sa masonry discussion.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang clue ay units bonded with mortar like clay, stone, or concrete units, ang sagot ay masonry.

question: A hollow masonry unit with two or three cells or cores made from Portland cement, water, and various types of aggregates:
keyMemory: Kapag cement-based hollow masonry unit with cores, sagot mo ay CHB.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. concrete hollow block
Ulitin natin: concrete hollow block.
Ito, very direct definition ito ng CHB.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Tingnan mo ang clues:
•	hollow masonry unit
•	may two or three cells or cores
•	gawa sa Portland cement, water, and aggregates
Iyan na iyan mismo ang concrete hollow block o CHB.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. structural brick – brick ito, pero hindi ito ang usual hollow cement-based unit na dini-describe dito.
•	c. structural clay block – made from clay family ito, hindi Portland cement with aggregates.
•	d. brick – masyadong general, at hindi sakto sa concrete hollow unit definition.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo habang nagsasagot:
1.	May Portland cement ba sa stem? Oo.
2.	So tanggal agad ang clay-based choices.
3.	Hollow ba ang description na may cells/cores? Oo.
4.	Kaya ang natitirang pinaka-eksakto ay concrete hollow block.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay yung generic na brick.
Minsan kapag “masonry unit” ang narinig, brick agad ang unang pumapasok sa isip. Pero ang tanong mismo nagsabi ng Portland cement + aggregates, so hindi brick ang tamang family.
Philippine context
Sa Philippine practice, CHB ang napaka-common na masonry unit, kaya high-yield ito sa ALE.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag cement-based hollow masonry unit with cores, sagot mo ay CHB.

visualSourceRefs.label: CMHA / NCMA — Concrete Masonry Technical References

visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Purpose of CHB cells
keyMemory: Ang pangunahing dahilan ng CHB cells ay para gumaan ang block.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. To make it lightweight
Ulitin natin: to make it lightweight.
Ito, gusto ko itong i-clear nang mabuti, kasi may ibang choices na parang puwede ring secondary effects. Pero ang tanong ay purpose sa pinaka-basic sense.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang pangunahing dahilan kung bakit may cells or cores ang CHB ay para:
•	mabawasan ang dami ng concrete material
•	bumaba ang bigat
•	maging mas practical ang handling at installation
So ang pinakaunang at pinaka-basic answer ay to make it lightweight.
Bakit hindi “to make it stronger”?
Hindi primary purpose ang paglalagay ng cells para palakasin siya.
Sa totoo lang, kung solid lahat iyan, mas mabigat siya at puwedeng mas solid ang mass, pero hindi iyon ang practical masonry unit design.
Bakit hindi “to make it cheaper”?
Puwedeng secondary result ito dahil mas kaunting material ang gamit. Pero hindi ito ang pinakamahusay na technical answer sa tanong na purpose.
Bakit hindi “to allow heat insulation”?
May thermal benefit din ang voids, oo. Pero again, secondary effect ito. Ang primary at pinaka-obvious design reason ay weight reduction.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Tanungin mo sarili mo: “Ano ang pinakaunang effect ng may hollow cores?”
Bumababa ang bigat.
2.	‘Yung cheaper at heat insulation, puwedeng kasunod na benefits lang.
3.	Kapag may primary and secondary effects sa choices, piliin mo ang primary technical reason.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay heat insulation, kasi may air space nga naman. Tama naman as added benefit.
Pero sa ALE, kapag may mas basic engineering answer, iyon ang dapat piliin.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Ang pangunahing dahilan ng CHB cells ay para gumaan ang block.

visualSourceRefs.label: CMHA / NCMA — Concrete Masonry Technical References

visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: A generic term that refers to any of a number of cementitious substances that are applied to a surface in paste form after which it hardens into a solid material:
keyMemory: Kapag generic surface-applied cementitious paste finish ang tanong, ang sagot ay plaster.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Plaster
Ulitin natin: Plaster.
Ito, ang keyword dito ay generic term at applied to a surface in paste form.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang plaster ay general term para sa material na:
•	ina-apply sa surface habang paste-like or workable pa
•	pagkatapos ay tumitigas
•	ginagamit bilang finish or coating sa walls and ceilings
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Gypsum – material/component ito, pero hindi generic term para sa whole applied finish system
•	b. Concrete – hindi ito surface-applied paste finish sa context ng tanong
•	d. Mortar – bonding/bedding material ito more than general surface finish term
Elimination method
Ganito ang isipin mo:
1.	Surface application ba ang usapan? Oo.
2.	Paste form na titigas? Oo.
3.	Generic term ba ang hinahanap, hindi specific ingredient? Oo.
4.	Kaya plaster, hindi gypsum.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay gypsum, kasi plastering-related material nga siya.
Pero tandaan:
Gypsum is one material. Plaster is the generic surface-applied finish term.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag generic surface-applied cementitious paste finish ang tanong, ang sagot ay plaster.

question: This is a proportioned mixture of cement, fine aggregate and water:
keyMemory: Cement + fine aggregate + water = cement mortar.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Cement Mortar
Ulitin natin: Cement Mortar.
Ito, classic definition item ulit.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang cement mortar ay mixture ng:
•	cement
•	fine aggregate
•	water
Wala pang coarse aggregate, kaya hindi pa ito concrete.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Concrete – may coarse aggregate dapat ito
•	c. Lean Concrete – concrete pa rin ito, just with lean mix proportion
•	d. CHB – finished masonry unit ito, hindi mixture definition
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Fine aggregate lang ba ang binanggit? Oo.
2.	So hindi ito concrete.
3.	Ang pinaka-sakto ay cement mortar.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay concrete dahil familiar ang word.
Pero ulitin natin:
No coarse aggregate = not concrete.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Cement + fine aggregate + water = cement mortar.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: This is a mortar of cementitious material (lime, gypsum or cement), sand and water which is applied in coats (layers) to masonry surfaces, lath or various types of plaster board to give a hard finish surface to interior or exterior walls and ceilings:
keyMemory: Kapag walls/ceilings finish applied in coats, ang tamang term ay plaster.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Plaster
Ulitin natin: Plaster.
Ito, mas detailed definition ito ng plaster.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang plaster ay:
•	gawa sa cementitious material tulad ng lime, gypsum, or cement
•	may sand at water
•	ina-apply in coats or layers
•	para magbigay ng hard finished surface sa walls and ceilings
Eksaktong eksakto ang description.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Plasterette – hindi ito ang general standard term na hinihingi ng stem
•	c. Cement Mortar – mas general mixture definition ito, pero kulang sa specific finish-application meaning ng item
•	d. Scratchcoat – first coat lang ito ng plaster system, hindi buong material/process name
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	May clue bang applied in coats/layers to walls and ceilings? Oo.
2.	Hindi lang mixture ang tanong, kundi finish application system
3.	Kaya mas tama ang plaster kaysa plain cement mortar
4.	Scratchcoat? part lang iyon, hindi whole answer
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang cement mortar.
Bakit? Kasi tama naman na may cementitious material, sand, and water.
Pero ang hinahanap dito ay iyong surface finish material applied in coats, at iyon ay plaster.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag walls/ceilings finish applied in coats, ang tamang term ay plaster.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: These types of brick have smooth outer surface with a dull satin or high gloss finish, are load bearing, fire resisting, and impervious and are usually formed with vertical hollow cores through the body with scoring on the back:
keyMemory: Kapag ang brick ay may smooth satin or glossy impervious face, isipin mo glazed brick.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Glazed Brick
Ulitin natin: Glazed Brick.
Ito, medyo descriptive item ito kaya magandang himayin.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang pinaka-strong clue dito ay:
•	smooth outer surface
•	dull satin or high gloss finish
•	impervious
Iyan ang signature ng glazed brick.
Bakit hindi structural brick?
Ang structural brick ay puwedeng load-bearing, oo. Pero ang clue na high gloss or satin finish at impervious finish ang mas nagdadala sa glazed brick.
Bakit hindi common brick?
Ang common brick ay hindi kilala sa ganitong refined finish characteristics.
Bakit hindi fire brick?
Ang fire brick ay para sa high-temperature service, hindi para sa glossy surfaced wall-face description.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Surface finish ba ang pinaka-highlight? Oo.
2.	May gloss/satin ba sa description? Oo.
3.	Kapag may glossy impervious outer finish, sagot ay glazed brick.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay structural brick, kasi sinabi ring load-bearing and fire-resisting.
Pero huwag mong kalimutan ang pinaka-unique clue:
smooth glazed finish.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang brick ay may smooth satin or glossy impervious face, isipin mo glazed brick.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: A kind of brick used for high temperature
keyMemory: High temperature brick = firebrick.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Firebrick
Ulitin natin: Firebrick.
Ito, direct item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang firebrick ay specially made para sa:
•	furnaces
•	kilns
•	fireplaces
•	high-temperature exposure
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Fly-ash brick – masonry unit type ito, hindi specifically refractory high-temperature brick
•	b. Thermal brick – vague term, hindi ito ang standard answer
•	d. Cinder block – concrete masonry unit ito, hindi pang high-temperature refractory service
Elimination method
1.	Clue: high temperature
2.	Hanapin ang standard refractory term
3.	Sagot: firebrick
Common trap
Walang masyadong trap dito.
Kapag high-temperature service, firebrick agad.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
High temperature brick = firebrick.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Standard size of a common brick
keyMemory: Sa reviewer item na ito, ang standard size of common brick ay 3 ¾ x 2 1/2 x 8 inches.
explanation:
Best answer: a. 3 ¾ x 2 1/2 x 8"
Ulitin natin: 3 ¾ x 2 1/2 x 8 inches.
Ito, aaminin ko, medyo magulo ang item dahil may iba't ibang standard brick dimensions depende sa system, country, at actual vs nominal sizing. Pero sa reviewer logic at sa choices na ibinigay, ang best answer ay 3 ¾ x 2 1/2 x 8 inches.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
Sa old reviewer/building materials memory items, iyan ang karaniwang lumalabas na common brick size.
At sa mga choices, ito rin ang pinaka-plausible at pinaka-klasikong dimension set.
Bakit kailangan maging maingat dito?
Kasi sa actual practice at references, may variation sa:
•	actual size
•	nominal size
•	modular vs non-modular brick
•	country standard
So ito iyong tanong na hindi ko sasabihing universally fixed sa lahat ng context. Pero for ALE reviewer answering, letter a ang strongest answer.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. 3 ¼ x 2 ¼ x 8" – possible-looking pero hindi ito ang classic reviewer memory answer
•	c. 3 x 2 ½ x 8" – masyadong manipis/odd proportion
•	d. 3 1/8 x 2 ½ x 8" – less standard-looking in this reviewer context
Elimination method
Ganito ang diskarte kapag hindi ka sure:
1.	Hanapin ang pinaka-classic proportion ng common brick
2.	Iwasan ang sobrang awkward or less common-looking dimensions
3.	Sa choices, ang pinaka-“standard reviewer” feel ay 3 ¾ x 2 1/2 x 8"
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay pag-a-assume na iisa lang ang brick size sa lahat ng bansa at systems.
Sa board review, ang mahalaga ay kung ano ang accepted reviewer answer sa item.
Philippine/ALE note
Brick questions sa ALE madalas galing sa reviewer conventions rather than live market inventory. Kaya tandaan mo ito as reviewer memory answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer item na ito, ang standard size of common brick ay 3 ¾ x 2 1/2 x 8 inches.

question: Together with wood and clay, also one of the basic building materials of man:
keyMemory: Sa mga basic ancient building materials, tandaan mo ang trio na: wood, clay, and stone.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Stone
Ulitin natin: Stone.
Ito, medyo historical at fundamental materials question ito.
Ang clue dito ay “basic building materials of man.”
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kung iisipin mo ang pinakaunang construction materials na ginamit ng tao, ang pinaka-basic at pinaka-ancient ay:
•	wood
•	clay
•	stone
Kaya sa choices, ang pinaka-natural na partner ng wood at clay ay stone.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Soil – malapit ito sa clay at earth construction, pero ang usual classic trio sa building materials history ay wood, clay, and stone. Hindi “wood, clay, and soil.”
•	c. Brick – ginawa lang ang brick mula sa clay; hindi ito kasing-basic or primitive as raw stone in the historical sense.
•	d. Cement – malinaw na mas modern processed material ito, hindi ancient basic material.
Elimination method
Ganito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Ang tanong ba ay historical/basic materials? Oo.
2.	Alin sa choices ang pinaka-primitive at pinakamatagal nang gamit?
Stone
3.	Brick and cement are processed materials, so puwede mong alisin.
4.	Soil is too broad; stone is the classic construction-material answer.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay soil, kasi parang basic din siya.
Pero tandaan mo: kapag reviewer wording na “with wood and clay”, ang usual accepted trio ay stone.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa mga basic ancient building materials, tandaan mo ang trio na: wood, clay, and stone.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Which among the following is not a classification of stone?
keyMemory: Igneous, sedimentary, metamorphic ang tatlong pangunahing classification. Obsidian is just one specific rock type.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Obsidian Rock
Ulitin natin: Obsidian Rock.
Ito, very important ang distinction between classification and example.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang three major classifications of rocks/stones are:
•	Igneous
•	Sedimentary
•	Metamorphic
Ang obsidian ay hindi classification. Isa itong specific type of igneous volcanic glass, not one of the three main categories.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Igneous Rock – classification ito
•	c. Metamorphic Rock – classification ito
•	d. Sedimentary Rock – classification ito
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Hanapin ang main rock classes na natutunan mo sa basic geology/building materials.
2.	Usually tatlo lang ang core:
o	igneous
o	sedimentary
o	metamorphic
3.	Kapag may isang specific rock name sa choices, malamang iyon ang hindi classification.
4.	Kaya Obsidian Rock ang outlier.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay dahil may “rock” din sa dulo ng obsidian, baka isipin mong kapantay lang siya ng ibang tatlo.
Pero huwag kang magpapadala sa tunog.
Ang tanong ay classification, hindi example.
ALE reasoning
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
“Category ba ito, o member lang ng category?”
Dito, obsidian is a member/example, not a category.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Igneous, sedimentary, metamorphic ang tatlong pangunahing classification.
Obsidian is just one specific rock type.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Igneous rock is formed by the crystallization of:
keyMemory: Igneous = galing sa cooling/crystallization ng molten magma.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. molten magma
Ulitin natin: molten magma.
Ito, basic geology foundation ito, pero napaka-high yield sa building materials.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang igneous rocks ay nabubuo sa paglamig at pag-crystallize ng molten rock material, which is magma kapag nasa loob ng earth, or lava kapag nasa surface.
So kapag tinanong ka:
“formed by crystallization of?”
automatic dapat: molten magma.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. shale – sedimentary rock na ito, hindi source ng igneous rock
•	c. sediments – ito ang source process ng sedimentary rocks
•	d. volcanic ash – puwedeng maging part ng pyroclastic deposits, pero hindi ito ang pinakabasic at tamang answer sa general formation question
Elimination method
Ganito ang thought process:
1.	If igneous, isipin mo agad fire / molten / cooled rock
2.	Tanggal ang anything related to sediments
3.	Between volcanic ash and molten magma, alin ang mas general at mas textbook definition?
Molten magma
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay volcanic ash dahil volcanic din naman siya.
Pero tandaan:
•	ang tanong ay general formation of igneous rock
•	ang textbook answer is crystallization of molten magma
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Igneous = galing sa cooling/crystallization ng molten magma.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: A stone that is cut into a specific size, 2 feet or more in length and squared to dimension and to a specific thickness is:
keyMemory: Kapag ang bato ay precisely cut and squared to dimensions, ang sagot ay dimension stone.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. dimension stone
Ulitin natin: dimension stone.
Ito, very descriptive item ito. Ang clue ay nasa wording mismo.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang dimension stone ay stone na:
•	cut to specific size
•	may controlled dimensions
•	may required thickness
•	ginagamit where exact sizing matters
Ang phrase na “squared to dimension” halos sinisigaw na ang sagot ay dimension stone.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. rubble stone – irregular at rough pieces ito, hindi accurately cut to precise dimensions
•	c. stone veneer – manipis na facing stone ito, but the question is more about the stone being cut and squared to exact size, which fits the broader term dimension stone
•	d. brick – ibang material family ito
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	May salitang specific size, squared, specific thickness
2.	Kapag precision-cut stone ang usapan, alisin mo agad ang rubble
3.	Brick is out because not stone in this context
4.	Between veneer and dimension stone, ang mas exact na definition ay dimension stone
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay stone veneer, kasi cut stone din naman iyon minsan.
Pero ang key distinction:
•	veneer = function as facing
•	dimension stone = classification based on being accurately cut to size
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang bato ay precisely cut and squared to dimensions, ang sagot ay dimension stone.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Stone delivered from the quarries in rough and irregular shape that has at least one good face for exposure in wall is called:
keyMemory: Rough and irregular quarry stone with one good exposed face = rubble stone.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. rubble stone
Ulitin natin: rubble stone.
Ito naman, kabaligtaran halos ng previous item.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang rubble stone ay:
•	rough
•	irregular
•	hindi precisely dressed like dimension stone
•	pero puwedeng may at least one presentable face for exposed wall work
Iyan ang exact character ng description.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. dimension stone – too regular and precisely cut
•	c. stone veneer – facing application ito, not the rough quarry-delivered irregular stone description
•	d. ashlar – dressed, cut, and more regular masonry stone ito; hindi rough irregular quarry form
Elimination method
Ganito ang diskarte:
1.	Hanapin ang words na rough at irregular
2.	Kapag rough/irregular, tanggal agad ang dimension stone at ashlar
3.	Veneer? not the best match
4.	Natitira ang rubble stone
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang ashlar, lalo na kung ang iniisip mo ay exposed face sa wall.
Pero tandaan:
•	Ashlar = dressed and regular
•	Rubble = rough and irregular
ALE reasoning
Ito ang reason bakit natitira ito sa choices:
Dahil ang tanong ay hindi about elegant finished stone masonry, kundi about rough quarry stone with one usable face.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Rough and irregular quarry stone with one good exposed face = rubble stone.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: This is a type of metamorphic rock, characterized by its vein structure which is actually a recrystallized limestone used for facades, floors and walls:
keyMemory: Recrystallized limestone with veining = marble.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Marble
Ulitin natin: Marble.
Ito, napakaganda ng clue words dito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang marble ay:
•	metamorphic rock
•	galing sa recrystallized limestone
•	kilala sa veining
•	widely used for facades, floors, and walls
Lahat ng clues, tumatama sa marble.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Travertine – common trap ito. Travertine is more commonly classified as sedimentary limestone-related stone, not metamorphic recrystallized limestone.
•	c. Argillite – metamorphic-related rock ito pero hindi ito ang classic veined recrystallized limestone for architectural finishes.
•	d. Sandstone – sedimentary rock ito, not metamorphic.
Elimination method
Ito ang dapat mong gawin:
1.	Hanapin ang first clue: metamorphic
2.	Second clue: recrystallized limestone
3.	Third clue: vein structure
4.	Kapag pinagsama mo iyang tatlo, marble agad.
Common trap
Pinaka-trap si travertine dahil ginagamit din siya sa walls and floors.
Pero ang killer clue ay recrystallized limestone.
Iyan ang marble, hindi travertine.
Philippine/ALE reasoning
Sa ALE, maraming tanong na ganyan: pareho silang finish stone, pero ang tamang sagot lalabas kapag nakita mo ang geologic identity, hindi lang appearance or application.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Recrystallized limestone with veining = marble.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: A building stone of igneous origin and composed of quartz, hornblende and mica is a:
keyMemory: Kapag igneous building stone with quartz, hornblende, and mica, ang sagot ay granite.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Granite
Ulitin natin: Granite.
Ito, classic stone identification ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang granite ay an igneous rock commonly composed of minerals like:
•	quartz
•	feldspar
•	mica
and may also include dark minerals like hornblende
So sa choices, ito ang pinaka-tamang mineral composition match.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Limestone – sedimentary rock ito, not igneous
•	b. Slate rock – metamorphic rock ito
•	d. Serpentine – igneous or metamorphic association can be discussed depending on context, but hindi ito ang classic stone described by quartz, hornblende, and mica mix in the reviewer sense
Elimination method
Ganito ang step-by-step:
1.	Unang clue: igneous origin
2.	So tanggal agad ang limestone at slate
3.	Natira: granite and serpentine
4.	Alin ang mas kilalang composition na may quartz + mica + hornblende?
Granite
Common trap
Minsan ang examinee napipili ang serpentine kasi tunog stone finish material din siya.
Pero sa mineral composition clue, granite ang mas exact na answer.
ALE reasoning
Kapag rock identification ang tanong, huwag lang sa application tumingin.
Tingnan mo ang:
•	classification
•	mineral composition
•	geologic origin
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag igneous building stone with quartz, hornblende, and mica, ang sagot ay granite.

Plaster Block
4.	Clay at shale tanggal agad dahil maling material family
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay kung mabasa mo lang ang word na block at hindi mo pinansin ang gypsum.
Sa ALE, laging sundan ang material clue, hindi lang ang object name.

Kapag gypsum partition block ang description, ang sagot ay Plaster Block.

question: Steel bars used for reinforcement of concrete are:
keyMemory: Sa reviewer logic, steel bars for concrete reinforcement are called billet steel bars.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. billet steel bars
Ulitin natin: billet steel bars.
Ito, napakahalaga ng wording ng reviewer note sa ibaba ng page. Nakasulat doon na ang reinforcement bars for concrete are called billet-steel bars or rail-steel bars.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa context ng concrete reinforcement, ang tamang term sa choices ay billet steel bars.
Ito ang recognized material category for reinforcing bars sa ganitong reviewer framing.

•	c. zee bars – steel section/shape ito, hindi standard reinforcing bar answer
•	d. tee bars – same, section/shape ito, not the common rebar classification here
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:

Reinforcing bars.

3.	Between “structural steel bars” and “billet steel bars,” alin ang mas exact at supported ng page note?
Billet steel bars
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay structural steel bars kasi parang tunog tama.
Pero tandaan:
Kapag may general term at may mas specific technical term, piliin mo ang specific.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer logic, steel bars for concrete reinforcement are called billet steel bars.

question: What is the standard weight of a 25 mm diameter hot-rolled weldable deformed or plain steel bar per meter length?
keyMemory: Para sa 25 mm bar, ang weight ay about 3.85 kg/m.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. 3.853 kilos
Ulitin natin: 3.853 kilos per meter.
Ito, puwede mo itong sagutin kahit hindi mo kabisado ang table, basta alam mo ang quick formula.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang standard estimate ng weight per meter ng steel bar ay:
Weight per meter = d² / 162
Kung 25 mm:
•	25² = 625
•	625 / 162 = about 3.86 kg/m
Ang pinakamalapit na choice ay 3.853 kilos.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. 343 kilos – obvious na absurdly mataas; halatang mali ang unit sense
•	c. 4.833 kilos – masyadong mabigat para sa 25 mm per meter
•	d. 2.466 kilos – masyadong magaan; ito mas malapit sa mas maliit na bar size
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Gamitin ang formula: d² / 162
2.	Estimate:
o	25 mm bar should be around 4 kg/m, but slightly below
3.	Kaya ang sagot ay iyong nasa 3.8+ range
4.	So 3.853 kg ang natitira
Common trap
Minsan natatakot ang examinee sa computation. Pero simple lang ito.
At kahit di mo ma-compute nang exact, puwede kang mag-estimate.
ALE tip
Magandang kabisaduhin mo ang formula na ito:
kg/m = d² / 162
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Para sa 25 mm bar, ang weight ay about 3.85 kg/m.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: What is the standard weight of a 16 mm diameter hot-rolled weldable deformed or plain steel bar per meter length?
keyMemory: 16 mm bar ≈ 1.58 kg/m.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. 1.578 kilos
Ulitin natin: 1.578 kilos per meter.
Ito, same method lang sa previous item.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Using the same formula:
Weight per meter = d² / 162
For 16 mm:
•	16² = 256
•	256 / 162 = about 1.58 kg/m
So ang tamang choice ay 1.578 kilos.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. 0.888 kilos – masyadong magaan; mas bagay ito sa mas maliit na bar
•	c. 1.872 kilos – medyo mataas para sa 16 mm
•	d. 2.466 kilos – masyadong mabigat; pang mas malaking diameter ito
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	16 mm bar is not tiny, not huge
2.	So weight per meter should be around 1.5 to 1.6 kg
3.	Kaya ang pinakamalapit ay 1.578 kg
Common trap
Pinagpapalit minsan ang 16 mm at 20 mm values.
Kaya maganda may rough sense ka:
•	16 mm ≈ 1.58 kg/m
•	20 mm ≈ 2.47 kg/m
•	25 mm ≈ 3.85 kg/m
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
16 mm bar ≈ 1.58 kg/m.

question: A metal use for cookware because of its heat retention properties.
keyMemory: Kapag cookware because of heat retention, sagot ay cast iron.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Cast Iron
Ulitin natin: Cast Iron.
Ito, practical materials behavior question ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang cast iron ay kilala sa mahusay na heat retention.
Kaya common ito sa:
•	frying pans
•	skillets
•	pots
•	cookware na gusto mo ng steady, retained heat
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Wrought iron – ductile and corrosion-resistant-ish traditional material, pero hindi ito ang classic cookware answer for heat retention
•	c. Flat iron – hindi ito material classification in the same sense; tool/object wording ito
•	d. Aluminum – mabilis uminit pero hindi kasing ganda ng cast iron pagdating sa heat retention
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Ang clue ay heat retention, hindi just heat conduction
2.	Aluminum heats fast, yes, pero hindi iyon ang strong heat-retention answer
3.	Cast iron ang classic cookware material for retained heat
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay aluminum kasi common din sa kusina.
Pero tandaan:
•	Aluminum = mabilis uminit, lightweight
•	Cast iron = mabigat, matagal mag-hold ng init
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag cookware because of heat retention, sagot ay cast iron.

question: Brand name for lath is:

So among the visible choices, ang brand name for lath ay HYRIB.

•	b. STEELCRETE – ito ay example ng expanded mesh, hindi lath

o	STEELCRETE = expanded mesh
o	HYRIB = lath
3.	Kaya malinaw ang sagot

Ang trap dito ay STEELCRETE dahil metal-related din ang tunog.

question: If shadow line was introduced, what will be the ideal to use?
keyMemory: Sa poorly worded item na ito, ang best answer ay metal inserts for shadow line introduction.
explanation:
Best answer: a. Metal inserts
Ulitin natin: Metal inserts.
Aaminin ko, medyo awkward ang wording ng item. Pero among the choices, ang best-fit answer ay metal inserts.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
Kapag ang usapan ay shadow line sa plaster or finishing detail, ang idea ay magkaroon ng intentional reveal or groove line.
Sa ganitong reviewer context, ang pinaka-generic at pinaka-defensible answer ay metal inserts, dahil sila ang ginagamit para ma-form o ma-maintain ang reveal/shadow line.
Bakit hindi U-channel?
Ito ang malakas na trap.
Oo, sa ibang modern detailing, puwedeng gumamit ng channel profiles. Pero ang item ay hindi malinaw na gypsum-board edge trim detail ang context.
Sa mas generic plaster/reveal wording, metal inserts ang mas safe at mas broad-correct answer.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Tubular Section – hindi ito ang normal reveal/shadow line forming answer
•	c. Flat Bar – puwedeng formwork/fabrication accessory lang ang dating, hindi ideal standard answer
•	d. U-channel – possible in some systems, but not the strongest general answer here
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Ang tanong ba ay generic reveal/shadow line treatment? Oo
2.	Hanapin ang pinaka-general at pinaka-purpose-fit material
3.	Metal inserts is the best fit
4.	Huwag pumili ng mas system-specific choice kung hindi naman malinaw ang context
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang U-channel dahil mukhang technical at architectural detail-like.
Pero sa magulong question, piliin mo ang broadest defensible correct answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa poorly worded item na ito, ang best answer ay metal inserts for shadow line introduction.

question: What is a steel element such as wire, cable, bar, rod or strand or a bundle of such elements, used to impart prestress to concrete?
keyMemory: Sa prestressed concrete, the steel element is called a tendon.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Tendons
Ulitin natin: Tendons.
Ito, standard prestressed concrete terminology ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa prestressed concrete, ang element used to impart prestress is called a tendon.
Puwede itong:
•	wire
•	strand
•	bar
•	cable
•	bundle of these
Eksaktong-eksakto sa definition.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Tenon cables – hindi ito ang standard prestressing term
•	b. Prestressing cables – too narrow or non-standard compared with the formal term
•	d. Reinforcements – too broad; not all reinforcement is prestressing element
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Clue word: impart prestress
2.	Hanapin ang formal technical term
3.	Sagot: tendon
4.	Huwag pumili ng general or awkward non-standard wording
Common trap
May pipili ng prestressing cables dahil parang direct English equivalent.
Pero sa engineering terminology, ang tamang umbrella term ay tendon.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa prestressed concrete, the steel element is called a tendon.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: A commercially pure iron of fibrous nature, valued for its corrosion resistance and ductility is
keyMemory: Fibrous and ductile commercially pure iron = wrought iron.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Wrought iron
Ulitin natin: Wrought iron.
Ito, classic metallurgy item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang wrought iron ay kilala sa:
•	fibrous nature
•	pagiging ductile
•	relatively better corrosion resistance
•	workability
Iyan ang signature description niya.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Cast iron – brittle ito relative to wrought iron; hindi fibrous
•	c. Black iron – mas commercial/common naming context ito, hindi iyong exact metallurgical description na hinahanap
•	d. Pig iron – crude raw iron product ito, hindi refined fibrous ductile material
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Hanapin ang clue: fibrous
2.	Another clue: ductility
3.	Kapag fibrous + ductile iron ang description, wrought iron agad
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay cast iron kasi familiar siyang iron type.
Pero tandaan:
•	Cast iron = brittle, high compressive strength
•	Wrought iron = fibrous, ductile
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Fibrous and ductile commercially pure iron = wrought iron.

question: An iron alloy usually including carbon and silicon which has high compressive strength but low tensile strength is
keyMemory: High compressive, low tensile iron alloy = cast iron.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Cast iron
Ulitin natin: Cast iron.
Ito, opposite feel siya ng previous question.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang cast iron ay iron alloy with relatively high carbon content, and commonly associated with:
•	high compressive strength
•	low tensile strength
•	brittleness compared with wrought iron
So very exact ang description.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Wrought iron – ductile ito, hindi iyan ang description
•	c. Black iron – not the precise metallurgical answer here
•	d. Pig iron – raw product/base material ito, not the final engineering-use answer intended by the question
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	High compressive but low tensile?
That sounds like a brittle iron
2.	So alisin ang wrought iron
3.	Sa natira, ang classic answer ay cast iron
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay pig iron dahil high-carbon raw iron din ang dating.
Pero tandaan:
Kapag actual material property question na common engineering answer, cast iron ang expected.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
High compressive, low tensile iron alloy = cast iron.

question: A metal that is slowly oxidized when it is exposed to the atmosphere for long periods of time, forming a blue-green coating called a patina.
keyMemory: Blue-green patina = copper.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Copper
Ulitin natin: Copper.
Ito, very visual memory item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang copper kapag na-expose nang matagal sa atmosphere, nagfo-form ng blue-green patina.
Ito iyong nakikita mo sa old copper roofs, domes, statues, and claddings.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Brass – alloy ito, at hindi ito ang classic answer sa blue-green architectural patina item
•	c. Iron – rust formation usually reddish-brown, not blue-green patina
•	d. Tin – hindi ito ang classic patina answer sa architecture/building materials context
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Blue-green coating?
2.	Ano ang classic metal na kilala diyan?
Copper
3.	Iron rust is reddish-brown, so tanggal agad si iron
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay brass, dahil copper alloy din ito.
Pero kapag board-type item na blue-green patina, ang first and best answer is copper.
Philippine/ALE reasoning
Ito ay high-yield architectural materials association. Madalas lumalabas ang copper sa cladding, flashing, roofing, and ornamental metal questions.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Blue-green patina = copper.

question: Made with chromium or a combination of nickel and chromium used for building’s exterior wall panels, frames for doors, expansion joints, flashings, copings, fascia and gravel stops.
keyMemory: Kapag ang clue ay chromium at corrosion-resistant exterior applications, ang sagot ay stainless steel.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Stainless steel
Ulitin natin: Stainless steel.
Ito, ang clue dito ay sobrang importante:
“made with chromium or a combination of nickel and chromium.”
Iyan na mismo ang identity ng stainless steel.
Kaya bago ka pa tumingin sa application, halos lumalabas na ang sagot.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang stainless steel ay steel na may chromium, at madalas may kasama ring nickel, para magkaroon ng:
•	mataas na corrosion resistance
•	magandang appearance
•	suitability sa exposed exterior applications
Kaya swak na swak ito sa mga gamit na binanggit:
•	exterior wall panels
•	flashings
•	copings
•	fascia
•	frames
•	gravel stops
Lahat iyan exposed sa weather, so ang hanap mo ay corrosion-resistant steel.
At doon pumapasok si stainless steel.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Alloy steels – common trap ito.
Totoo naman na stainless steel is technically an alloy steel. Pero masyadong broad itong choice.
Ang tanong ay humihingi ng specific steel type made with chromium or nickel-chromium. Kaya hindi sapat ang generic na “alloy steels.”
•	c. Nickel steel – may nickel, yes, pero kulang.
Ang clue ay chromium or nickel + chromium. Mas direct pa rin iyan sa stainless steel.
•	d. Manganese steel – ibang gamit ito, more on abrasion resistance and impact-heavy use, hindi sa exposed architectural sheet applications.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo habang nagsasagot:
1.	Hanapin ang pinakamalakas na clue:
chromium or nickel + chromium
2.	Anong steel ang pinaka-kilala diyan?
Stainless steel
3.	Tanggal ang generic answer na alloy steels kung may mas exact answer
4.	Tanggal ang manganese steel dahil hindi iyon weather-exposed sheet application answer
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay alloy steels.
Bakit? Kasi technically tama ring alloy ang stainless.
Pero sa board exam, kapag may general term at may specific exact term, ang piliin mo ay specific.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang clue ay chromium at corrosion-resistant exterior applications, ang sagot ay stainless steel.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: A ferrous metal that offers great resistance to abrasion and finds important use in the cutting edges of heavy digging tools is a
keyMemory: Kapag abrasion resistance at heavy digging tool edges ang usapan, manganese steel ang sagot.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Manganese steel
Ulitin natin: Manganese steel.
Ito, ang pinakamalaking clue ay great resistance to abrasion.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang manganese steel ay kilala sa:
•	mataas na toughness
•	abrasion resistance
•	impact resistance
Kaya ginagamit ito sa mga pyesang exposed sa matinding wear tulad ng:
•	cutting edges
•	pandurog/crusher parts
•	heavy-duty tools
•	digging and earth-moving components
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Alloy steels – broad term lang ito, not the best exact answer
•	b. Weathering steel – ito ay para sa atmospheric corrosion resistance, hindi abrasion-heavy cutting edges
•	c. Nickel steel – ginagamit sa stronger structural applications, but not the classic answer for heavy abrasion cutting edges
•	d. Manganese steel – ito ang exact fit
Elimination method
Ganito ang diskarte:
1.	Clue word: abrasion
2.	Another clue: cutting edges of heavy digging tools
3.	Ang steel na kilala sa wear and impact resistance ay manganese steel
4.	Weathering steel? mali agad dahil corrosion/weather exposure ang association nun
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay alloy steels, kasi parang safe answer.
Pero again, safe-sounding broad answer is often wrong if may mas exact na choice.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag abrasion resistance at heavy digging tool edges ang usapan, manganese steel ang sagot.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: A brown film on the surface of bronze or similar metals, produced by oxidation over a long period.
keyMemory: Kapag bronze or copper surface film from long oxidation, ang tawag ay patina.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Patina
Ulitin natin: Patina.
Ito, visual memory item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang patina ay natural surface film na nabubuo sa metals tulad ng:
•	bronze
•	copper
•	similar nonferrous metals
Habang tumatagal, dahil sa oxidation and weathering, nagkakaroon sila ng characteristic surface layer. Iyan ang patina.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Rust – para ito sa iron or steel oxidation, usually reddish-brown. Hindi ito ang tamang term for bronze surface film.
•	b. Tinta – hindi ito standard technical answer dito
•	d. Corrosion – masyadong broad process term ito. Ang hinahanap ng tanong ay iyong specific film/result, hindi generic process
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Bronze ba ang metal? Oo.
2.	Hanap ba ay specific surface film term? Oo.
3.	Rust is for iron, so tanggal agad
4.	General term na corrosion? too broad
5.	Natitira: patina
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay corrosion dahil technically result nga naman ito ng oxidation.
Pero tanong kasi ay hindi “what process.”
Ang tanong ay what is the film called.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag bronze or copper surface film from long oxidation, ang tawag ay patina.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Also known as Corten Steel
keyMemory: Corten Steel = Weathering Steel.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Weathering Steel
Ulitin natin: Weathering Steel.
Ito, direct equivalency item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang Corten steel ay kilala bilang weathering steel dahil:
•	designed ito para bumuo ng protective rust-like surface
•	mas lumalaban ito sa atmospheric corrosion kaysa ordinary carbon steel
•	often used exposed without conventional painting in certain applications
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Manganese Steel – abrasion/wear steel ito, hindi Corten
•	b. Core Tensile Steel – hindi standard equivalent term
•	d. Carbon Steel – sobrang broad; weathering steel is a special type, not just plain carbon steel answer in this context
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Alam mo ba ang “Corten”? Kung oo, diretso sa weathering steel
2.	Kung hindi, hanapin ang choice na pinakakonektado sa weather exposure
3.	Kaya Weathering Steel
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay carbon steel dahil steel pa rin naman siya.
Pero tandaan:
Ang tanong ay alias or also-known-as item. So kailangan mo ang exact equivalent term.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Corten Steel = Weathering Steel.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Used to protect corners of concrete column.
keyMemory: Kapag protection ng corner ng concrete column, ang sagot ay corner guard.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Corner Guard
Ulitin natin: Corner Guard.
Ito, practical architectural/construction item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang corner guard ang tamang term para sa protection ng corners ng:
•	columns
•	wall edges
•	vulnerable protruding corners
Ginagamit ito para maiwasan ang:
•	chipping
•	impact damage

Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Corner Trim – puwedeng finishing or decorative trim ang dating nito, hindi necessarily protective
•	c. Corner Bumper – mas informal at hindi ito ang standard building term
•	d. Edge Guard – generic at puwedeng gamitin sa iba’t ibang edges, pero hindi ito ang most exact common term for column corners

Ganito ang isipin mo:
1.	Ang function ba ay protection, hindi decoration? Oo.

3.	Corner Guard ang pinakasakto
4.	Corner trim sounds finish-related lang
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay corner trim, lalo na kung iniisip mo finishing detail.
Pero dahil ang clue ay protect, dapat mas functional/protective term ang hanapin mo.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag protection ng corner ng concrete column, ang sagot ay corner guard.

question: The metal that has the highest electrical and thermal conductivity and is ductile, malleable and non-magnetic is:
keyMemory: Sa choices na ito, ang pinakamahusay na conductor na ductile at non-magnetic ay copper.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Copper
Ulitin natin: Copper.
Importanteng linaw: sa science, silver ang pinakamataas sa electrical conductivity, pero wala sa choices.
So among the given options, ang tamang sagot ay copper.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang copper ay kilala sa:
•	very high electrical conductivity
•	very high thermal conductivity
•	ductility
•	malleability
•	non-magnetic behavior
Kaya ginagamit ito sa:
•	wiring
•	conductors
•	flashing
•	gutters
•	roofing details
•	various metal works
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Tin – soft and useful for coatings/alloys, pero hindi ito ang top conductor among the choices
•	b. Lead – mababa ang conductivity relative to copper; soft and heavy
•	d. Aluminum – magaan at good conductor din, pero hindi kasing taas ng copper sa conductivity
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Clue: highest electrical and thermal conductivity
2.	Among the options, alin ang classic conductor metal?
Copper
3.	Aluminum is tempting, but copper is better conductor
4.	Lead and tin are easy eliminations
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang aluminum, kasi ginagamit din naman sa electrical applications.
Pero tandaan:
•	Aluminum = lighter, cheaper in some uses
•	Copper = better conductor
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa choices na ito, ang pinakamahusay na conductor na ductile at non-magnetic ay copper.

question: Standard gauge of tie wires?

Ito, very practical Philippine construction item ito.

Sa local estimating and site practice, #16 tie wire ang napaka-common na reference.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa ALE/reviewer and common site practice, tie wire = Gauge 16.

Sa reviewer/local estimating convention, 1 kilo of tie wire is taken as approximately 53 meters.

question: A composition of two or more metals fused together usually to obtain a desired property
keyMemory: Kapag ang tanong ay pinaghalong metals para sa desired property, ang sagot ay alloy.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Alloy
Ulitin natin: Alloy.
Ito, very basic pero high-yield materials definition ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang alloy ay kombinasyon ng dalawa o higit pang metallic elements, or minsan metal plus another element, para makuha ang desired properties tulad ng:
•	mas matigas
•	mas corrosion-resistant
•	mas durable
•	mas workable
So kapag sinabi ng tanong na “composition of two or more metals fused together”, almost direct definition na iyan ng alloy.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Annealing – process ito ng heat treatment, hindi material composition
•	c. Built-up – method or assembled form ito, hindi metallic composition term
•	d. Welded – joining process ito, hindi name ng resulting material composition
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Tanong ba ay process o material term?
Material term.
2.	Annealing and welded are processes, so tanggal agad.
3.	Built-up is assembly-related, hindi metallurgy term.
4.	Natitira: alloy
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay kapag napili ang welded dahil “pinagdugtong din naman ang metal.”
Pero tandaan:
Fused together as composition is different from joined together as pieces.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang tanong ay pinaghalong metals para sa desired property, ang sagot ay alloy.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Identify:
keyMemory: Kapag may adjustable tensioning body sa gitna, ang sagot ay turnbuckle.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. turnbuckle
Ulitin natin: turnbuckle.
Kahit medyo grainy ang image, identifiable pa rin siya.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang turnbuckle ay iyong fitting na may:
•	central body
•	threaded ends
•	ginagamit para i-adjust ang tension ng rods, wires, or cables
Sa larawan, makikita mo ang adjustable central body at iba't ibang end fittings. Iyan ang classic form ng turnbuckle.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. sagrod – ang sag rod ay usually simpleng rod used in roof framing/bracing, hindi itong adjustable fitting body
•	c. eye bolt – single bolt lang ito na may eye sa dulo, hindi whole adjustable assembly
•	d. weighing hook – hindi ito ang tamang hardware identity
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Hanapin sa image kung may central adjustable body
2.	Kapag meron, malamang turnbuckle
3.	Eye bolt is single-piece hardware lang
4.	Sag rod is rod, not this adjustable connector assembly
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay eye bolt kapag nakita mo lang iyong loop/eye portion.
Pero huwag kang tumingin sa isang dulo lang. Tingnan mo ang buong assembly.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag may adjustable tensioning body sa gitna, ang sagot ay turnbuckle.

question: A traditional building material that is easily worked, has durability and beauty and has freedom from rust and corrosion and is comparatively lightweight is:
keyMemory: Kapag ang tanong ay traditional material na magaan, madaling trabahuhin, at hindi kinakalawang, sagot ay wood.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Wood
Ulitin natin: Wood.
Ito, very descriptive material-character question ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang wood ay:
•	madaling i-work
•	relatively lightweight
•	may natural beauty
•	hindi “nagrarust”
•	historically one of the most traditional building materials
Iyan ang pinakamagandang fit sa buong description.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Stone – durable and beautiful, yes, pero hindi “easily worked” in the same practical sense at hindi rin comparatively lightweight
•	c. Metal – puwedeng easily fabricated in some ways, pero hindi ito “freedom from rust and corrosion” in general
•	d. Marble – maganda, yes, pero mabigat at hindi iyon ang broad traditional material description na hinahanap
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Clues:
o	easily worked
o	no rust
o	comparatively lightweight
2.	Tanggal agad ang stone and marble dahil mabibigat
3.	Tanggal ang metal dahil rust/corrosion is a common concern
4.	Natitira: wood
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay stone dahil traditional material din naman.
Pero ang killer clue ay comparatively lightweight.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang tanong ay traditional material na magaan, madaling trabahuhin, at hindi kinakalawang, sagot ay wood.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Heat gain through the structure of a habitable room occur in tropical regions at walls and roofs by conduction. This can be minimized by the use of
keyMemory: Kapag gusto mong bawasan ang heat conduction sa building envelope, wood is a better traditional answer than concrete or steel.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Wood
Ulitin natin: Wood.
Ito, ang logic dito ay tungkol sa thermal conductivity.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa choices, ang wood ang pinaka-practical building material na mababa ang thermal conductivity kumpara sa:
•	concrete
•	steel
At sa framing ng reviewer, ito ang mas accepted answer para makatulong mabawasan ang heat transfer by conduction sa walls and roofs.
Bakit hindi plastic?
Magandang trap ito.
Oo, maraming plastics ang poor conductors. Pero sa context ng building walls and roofs at traditional materials framing ng item, wood ang mas defensible at mas expected answer.
Bakit mali ang iba?
•	a. Concrete – mas mataas ang heat storage and conduction than wood
•	c. Steel – very conductive ito
•	d. Plastic – possible insulator in some forms, pero hindi ito ang strongest reviewer answer sa ganitong building-envelope context
Elimination method
Ganito ang isipin mo:
1.	Ang tanong ay heat gain by conduction
2.	So alisin mo ang mas conductive materials:
o	steel
o	concrete
3.	Between wood and plastic, alin ang mas accepted construction-material answer for walls/roofs?
Wood
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay plastic dahil iniisip ng iba, “insulator naman iyan.”
Tama naman in some contexts. Pero sa reviewer/building-material logic, wood ang mas standard answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag gusto mong bawasan ang heat conduction sa building envelope, wood is a better traditional answer than concrete or steel.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: The scientific name for wood is
keyMemory: Scientific name/plant tissue associated with wood = xylem.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. XYLEM
Ulitin natin: XYLEM.
Ito, direct terminology item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang xylem ang plant tissue responsible for water conduction and structural support, at ito ang siyang bumubuo sa wood portion ng puno.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. CITEM – hindi ito scientific name ng wood
•	c. APTG – hindi ito tamang botanical term dito
•	d. TANG – obvious distractor
Elimination method
Kapag may nakita kang familiar scientific/botanical term sa choices, at iyon lang ang mukhang legit, malaking clue iyon.
Dito, xylem agad ang lalabas.
Common trap
Wala masyado rito. Pure memory item lang ito.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Scientific name/plant tissue associated with wood = xylem.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Woods that come from the conifers which have needles for leaves are called:
keyMemory: Conifers with needle leaves = softwood.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. softwood
Ulitin natin: Softwood.
May partial repeated line sa taas na same thought lang, so ito ang full question na rereview natin.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang mga conifers na may needle-like leaves ay classified under softwoods sa building materials terminology.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. ironwood – specific common name ito, hindi general classification
•	b. mahogany – hardwood species ito, hindi conifer classification
•	d. hardwood – ito ay karaniwang from broadleaf deciduous trees, hindi needle-leaved conifers
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Key clue: conifers
2.	Another clue: needles for leaves
3.	Conifers = softwood
4.	Hardwood usually broadleaf trees
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay literal kang mag-isip na “matigas na kahoy” kaya hardwood.
Pero tandaan:
Softwood vs hardwood is botanical/classificatory first, not just literal hardness.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Conifers with needle leaves = softwood.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: If you want to splice two pieces of wood with the same material. What should you use?
keyMemory: Kapag wood ang sinasali at wood din ang pang-splice, ang sagot ay scab.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Scab
Ulitin natin: Scab.
Ito, practical carpentry/joinery item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang scab ay piraso rin ng kahoy na ikinakabit para ma-splice o ma-reinforce ang joint ng dalawang pirasong timber o wood member.
Ang clue dito ay “with the same material.”
Ibig sabihin, wood-to-wood reinforcement/joining piece ang hanap.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Batten – strip ito na kadalasang ginagamit to cover joints or hold boards, pero hindi ito ang best answer for splicing two wood pieces
•	c. Fish Plate – common trap ito. Madalas plate ito, often metal, used for joining members, pero dahil sinabi ng tanong same material, mas swak ang scab
•	d. Anchor – hindi ito ang splice answer
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Key clue: same material
2.	Kung kahoy ang pinag-uusapan, hanapin ang wood piece used to join wood
3.	Fish plate sounds technical, pero often not the same-material answer
4.	Kaya scab ang strongest answer
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang fish plate.
Bakit? Kasi known joining piece siya.
Pero the board trick is the phrase: “with the same material.”
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag wood ang sinasali at wood din ang pang-splice, ang sagot ay scab.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: What do you call trees that grow endwards?
keyMemory: Kapag ang tanong ay may typo pero halata ang concept, gamitin mo ang pinaka-logical intended pair. Dito, endogenous ang best-fit answer.
explanation:
Best answer: c. endogenous
Ulitin natin: Endogenous.
Aaminin ko, sablay ang wording ng item. Ang salitang “endwards” ay hindi standard. Malamang ang intended meaning nito ay inward/internal growth.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
Kung ang intended contrast ay:
•	exogenous = outward growth
•	endogenous = inward/internal growth
then ang best answer sa wording na ito ay endogenous.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. exogenous – ito ang magiging tama kung ang tanong ay “grow outwards”
•	b. softwoods – wood classification ito, hindi growth direction
•	d. hardwoods – same, classification ito, hindi growth pattern
Elimination method
Ito ang gawin mo sa board kapag may typo:
1.	Huwag mataranta
2.	Hanapin ang intended concept
3.	Dito, obvious na contrast pair ay exogenous vs endogenous
4.	Since “endwards” points toward endo, choose endogenous
Common trap
Kung i-assume mong typo ito for outwards, mapipili mo si exogenous.
Kaya mahalagang maging honest tayo rito: poorly worded item ito.
Pero based sa visible wording, endogenous ang best answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang tanong ay may typo pero halata ang concept, gamitin mo ang pinaka-logical intended pair. Dito, endogenous ang best-fit answer.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Green lumber is:

choices.text: lumber that still contains moisture or sap

answer: d. lumber that still contains moisture or sap
keyMemory: Green lumber = hindi pa tuyo, may moisture o sap pa.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. lumber that still contains moisture or sap
Ulitin natin: lumber that still contains moisture or sap.
Ito, classic wood terminology ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang green lumber ay kahoy na hindi pa seasoned or dried, kaya may natural moisture content pa.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. wolmanized lumber – treated lumber ito
•	b. creosote treated lumber – treated lumber din ito
•	c. talanized lumber – another treatment-related term, not green lumber
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	“Green” in lumber does not mean color
2.	It means fresh / not yet dried
3.	So hanapin ang option tungkol sa moisture or sap
4.	Answer: d
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay isipin na “green” means treated or environmentally friendly.
Hindi.
Sa lumber, green = basa pa / may moisture pa.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Green lumber = hindi pa tuyo, may moisture o sap pa.
