question: What part of the wood serves as the food of subterranean termites?
keyMemory: Termites feed on cellulose.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. cellulose
Ulitin natin: Cellulose.
Ito, very important materials-biology crossover item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang termites ay primarily kumakain ng cellulose, which is the main fibrous organic substance in wood.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. sapwood – mas vulnerable man ito madalas, pero ang tunay na pagkain nila sa chemical/material sense ay cellulose
•	c. heartwood – puwedeng attack-in ang wood parts depende sa conditions, pero hindi ito ang best scientific answer sa tanong
•	d. all of the above – mali dahil ang specific food substance talaga ay cellulose
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Tanong ba ay part/substance serving as food
2.	Not which part is more exposed, but what they actually digest
3.	Answer: cellulose
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay sapwood, dahil common knowledge na mas madaling tamaan iyon.
Pero again, hindi “most vulnerable zone” ang tanong.
Ang tanong ay food.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Termites feed on cellulose.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: What is the term applied to the operation performed in preparing wood for commercial purposes?
keyMemory: Kapag buong operation for preparing wood commercially ang usapan, lumbering ang best answer.
explanation:
Best answer: a. lumbering
Ulitin natin: Lumbering.
Ito, medyo malapit ang logging, kaya gusto ko itong linawin.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
Ang lumbering ang mas broad term for the operation/industry involved in preparing wood or timber for commercial use.
Kasama sa larger picture nito ang cutting, handling, and getting timber into usable commercial form.
Bakit hindi logging?
Magandang trap ito.
Ang logging is more specifically the cutting/felling and transporting of logs.
Mas makitid siya kaysa lumbering.
Bakit mali ang iba?
•	c. sawing – one step lang ito
•	d. drying – one step lang din ito
Elimination method
Ganito ang isipin mo:
1.	Tanong ba ay whole operation or one step lang?
Whole operation
2.	So alisin ang sawing and drying
3.	Between logging and lumbering, alin ang mas broad commercial-preparation term?
Lumbering
Common trap
Pinaka-trap si logging dahil mas familiar at mas tunog actual operation.
Pero ang board exam trick dito ay scope:
•	logging = mas specific
•	lumbering = mas broad
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag buong operation for preparing wood commercially ang usapan, lumbering ang best answer.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Usually 1 or 2 inches in rough thickness often containing knots or defects intended for use in mills making it into sash, doors and/or cabinets:
keyMemory: Kapag ang lumber ay for further millwork into doors, sash, and cabinets, ang sagot ay shop lumber.
explanation:
Correct answer: Shop lumber
Ulitin natin: Shop lumber.
Note lang: wala nang visible choices sa screenshot, pero based sa stem, ito ang tamang term.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang shop lumber ay lumber intended for remanufacture or further millwork into:
•	sash
•	doors
•	cabinets
•	other finished wood products
At tugma rin ang description na puwede itong may knots or defects dahil dadaan pa ito sa further processing and cutting.
Elimination method kahit walang choices
Kapag nakita mo ang clue na:
•	rough thickness
•	intended for mill use
•	gagawing sash, doors, cabinets
ang hinahanap na term ay shop lumber.
Common trap
Minsan nalilito ito sa finish lumber or select lumber.
Pero tandaan:
•	shop lumber = pang-remanufacture pa
•	hindi pa ito final finish grade product
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang lumber ay for further millwork into doors, sash, and cabinets, ang sagot ay shop lumber.

question: Wood that is surfaced by a planer on all four sides is known as
keyMemory: S4S = surfaced four sides. S1S = one side. S2S = two sides.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. S4S
Ulitin natin: S4S.
Ito, classic abbreviation question ito sa lumber terminology.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang S4S means Surfaced Four Sides.
Ibig sabihin, na-planer na o na-finish na ang kahoy sa apat na sides niya.
So sobrang direct ng tanong:
•	surfaced
•	by a planer
•	on all four sides
Kaya S4S agad.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. S1S – surfaced one side lang ito
•	c. rough lumber – ito ang opposite halos; sawed but not planed
•	d. S2S – two sides planed lang ito
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo habang sumasagot:
1.	Count the number in the abbreviation.
2.	Kung all four sides, hanapin mo ang may 4.
3.	Huwag masilaw sa familiar-looking abbreviations like S1S or S2S.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay S2S, lalo na kung nagmamadali ka at nakita mo lang ang word na “surfaced.”
Pero tandaan: hindi sapat na surfaced lang — basahin mo ang ilang sides.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
S4S = surfaced four sides.
S1S = one side.
S2S = two sides.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: This refers to the finished lumber which is further cut and processed at a lumber mill which includes doors, trim and other ornamental forms of wood:
keyMemory: Millwork ang broad term for processed finished wood items like doors, trim, and ornamental wood products.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Millwork
Ulitin natin: Millwork.
Ito, ang clue dito ay finished lumber further cut and processed at a lumber mill.
At kasama ang:
•	doors
•	trim
•	ornamental wood forms
Iyan ang language ng millwork.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang millwork ay broad term para sa manufactured wood finish items and building components na dumaan sa milling and shaping process, gaya ng:
•	doors
•	jambs
•	casings
•	trim
•	ornamental woodwork
•	interior finish components
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Moldings – subset lang ito ng millwork. Hindi lahat ng millwork ay moldings.
•	c. Cabinetry – specific category lang ito, hindi buong umbrella term.
•	d. Caps – masyadong narrow at hindi ito ang general answer.
Elimination method
Ganito ang diskarte:
1.	Tanong ba ay humihingi ng broad category o specific item?
Broad category.
2.	Kapag broad category ang tanong, piliin ang umbrella term.
3.	Dito, ang umbrella term ay millwork.
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang moldings, kasi ornamental wood form din naman iyon.
Pero tandaan:
Kung may mas malawak na tamang term, iyon ang pipiliin.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Millwork ang broad term for processed finished wood items like doors, trim, and ornamental wood products.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: General term for base board, cornices, balusters that are ornamental?
keyMemory: Kapag ornamental trim pieces tulad ng baseboards and cornices ang usapan, ang strongest answer ay moulding.
explanation:
Best answer: d. Moulding
Ulitin natin: Moulding.
Ito, medyo may konting awkwardness ang wording, pero ang best reviewer answer dito ay moulding.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
Kapag narinig mo ang:
•	base board
•	cornices
•	ornamental trim forms
ang pinaka-malakas na association diyan ay mouldings.
Ang moulding ay decorative/profiled wood trim used for finishing and ornamentation.
Bakit hindi millwork?
Ito ang common trap.
Ang millwork ay mas broad category.
Pero ang tanong ay hindi tungkol sa buong family ng processed wood items. Ang dating ng tanong ay mas specific sa ornamental trim forms. Kaya mas sakto si moulding.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Joinery – method/assembly of wood joints ito
•	b. Carpentry – trade/work classification ito
•	c. Millwork – broad category, pero hindi pinaka-specific answer rito
•	d. Moulding – pinaka-direct sa ornamental trim concept
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Ang item ba ay tungkol sa trade, method, o wood trim product?
2.	Joinery and carpentry are trade/method related, so tanggal agad.
3.	Between millwork and moulding, alin ang mas specific sa ornamental trim?
Moulding
Common trap
Kung broad ang isip mo, mapipili mo ang millwork.
Pero ALE trick ito:
Kapag may mas specific and exact answer, huwag sa broad answer pupunta.
Important note
Aaminin ko, medyo sablay ang inclusion ng balusters sa phrasing, kasi hindi lahat ng baluster ay tinatawag na moulding. Pero sa choices given, moulding pa rin ang best reviewer fit.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ornamental trim pieces tulad ng baseboards and cornices ang usapan, ang strongest answer ay moulding.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Wood usually used for jambs that are locally available.
keyMemory: Sa local ALE/reviewer logic, tanguile ang usual answer for jamb wood.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Tanguile
Ulitin natin: Tanguile.
Ito, Philippine materials familiarity item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa local practice, tanguile is a very common and practical choice for:
•	door jambs
•	frames
•	general joinery work
May good balance ito ng workability and availability.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Apitong – mas structural-heavy ang association nito
•	b. Palotsina – hindi ito ang pinaka-common jamb answer sa reviewer logic
•	d. Lauan – common sa plywood/paneling/general lighter wood use, pero hindi ito ang classic jamb answer dito
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Ang jamb ba ay framing/joinery application? Oo.
2.	Hanapin ang wood species na pinaka-associated sa local door jamb use.
3.	Tanguile ang lumalabas na practical reviewer answer.
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang lauan dahil very familiar wood name siya sa Pilipinas.
Pero pagiging familiar alone does not make it the best jamb answer.
Philippine context
Ito ang type ng item na kailangan may konting local market awareness ka, hindi lang textbook memory.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa local ALE/reviewer logic, tanguile ang usual answer for jamb wood.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Also known as Philippine ebony.
keyMemory: Philippine ebony = Kamagong.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Kamagong
Ulitin natin: Kamagong.
Ito, direct local wood-species identification ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang kamagong ang kilalang tinatawag na Philippine ebony dahil sa:
•	dark dense wood character
•	premium hardwood identity
•	ebony-like appearance and hardness association
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Tanguile – hindi ito Philippine ebony
•	b. Apitong – structural-use association ito
•	d. Lauan – common lighter-use wood family, not ebony association
Elimination method
Kapag may wood species alias question:
1.	Hanapin ang pinaka-premium, pinaka-dark dense hardwood association.
2.	Dito, malinaw na kamagong iyon.
Common trap
Wala masyadong trap dito kung kilala mo ang local species. Pure memory item ito.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Philippine ebony = Kamagong.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Wood grain where the annual rings run more or less parallel to the surface:
keyMemory: Annual rings parallel to the face = flat grain.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Flat grain
Ulitin natin: Flat grain.
Ito, very important lumber-cut orientation item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kapag ang annual rings ay more or less parallel to the surface, ang tawag ay flat grain.
Compare mo ito sa notes sa page:
•	Edge grain = annual rings approximately at right angle to the face
•	Angle grain = around 45 degrees
•	So parallel-ish to the surface = flat grain
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Edge grain – right angle to face, hindi parallel
•	b. Angle grain – 45 degrees, hindi parallel
•	d. Parallel grain – tunog tama, pero hindi ito ang standard term used here
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Hanapin ang relationship ng annual rings sa face/surface.
2.	Parallel to surface = not edge grain
3.	45 degrees = not angle grain
4.	The proper technical term among choices is flat grain
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang parallel grain dahil literal na kapareho ng wording ng stem.
Pero huwag padadala sa literal-English trap.
Hanapin mo ang standard lumber term.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Annual rings parallel to the face = flat grain.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Lumber that is subjected to pressure and injected with chemicals or salts to ensure it from rot is called:
keyMemory: Pressure + chemicals for rot protection = pressure treated lumber.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. pressure treated lumber
Ulitin natin: pressure treated lumber.
Ito, direct definition item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang pressure treated lumber ay kahoy na:
•	inilalagay under pressure
•	ini-inject ng preservatives/chemicals
•	para protektahan laban sa rot, decay, insects, and deterioration
Eksaktong tugma sa stem.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. kiln-dried lumber – drying lang ito, not preservative pressure treatment
•	c. air-dried lumber – natural drying lang din
•	d. all of the above – mali dahil hindi lahat ng drying method ay pressure chemical treatment
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Key words: pressure + injected with chemicals or salts
2.	Hindi ito drying question, preservation question ito
3.	Kaya tanggal agad ang kiln-dried at air-dried
4.	Natitira: pressure treated lumber
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay pagkapit sa word na “treated” tapos i-a-assume na pasok na ang kiln-dried.
Pero kiln drying is moisture reduction, not chemical pressure treatment.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Pressure + chemicals for rot protection = pressure treated lumber.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: What are the methods of artificial drying of timber?
keyMemory: Kapag valid ang lahat ng listed artificial drying methods, sagot ay all of the above.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. all of the above
Ulitin natin: all of the above.
Ito, magandang elimination question ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Lahat ng tatlong nasa choices ay artificial methods:
•	forced air drying
•	kiln drying
•	radio frequency dielectric drying
So kung ang tanong ay methods of artificial drying, at lahat sila valid, ang tamang sagot ay all of the above.
Bakit mali ang ibang individual choices?
Hindi naman sila “mali” by themselves. Kulang lang sila kung isa lang ang pipiliin mo, dahil lahat ay pasok.
Elimination method
Ganito ang diskarte:
1.	I-check isa-isa ang choices.
2.	Kung alam mong valid ang a, b, at c,
3.	Then all of the above becomes the strongest answer.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay kapag may isang familiar ka lang like kiln drying, tapos iyon agad pipiliin mo.
Pero ALE strategy: kapag may all of the above, validate mo muna each item.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?

question: Besides decay, what is another cause of defects in wood?
keyMemory: Kapag magulo ang item, piliin ang best-fit reviewer answer, at dito iyon ay dry rot.
explanation:
Best answer: b. dry rot
Ulitin natin: dry rot.
Aaminin ko rito: mahina ang wording ng item. Pero among the choices, ang best reviewer answer ay dry rot.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
Sa practical reviewer logic:
•	decay is one problem
•	dry rot is another commonly cited wood defect/problem term
Bakit hindi fungi?
Ito ang pinakamalaking trap.
Fungi is usually the organism or biological cause behind wood decay, hindi siya neatly “another defect” beside decay.
Parang cause siya more than separate defect name.
Bakit hindi deterioration?
Mas broad pa ito kaysa decay. Halos synonym/general condition lang ang dating, so mahina as exact answer.
Bakit hindi none?
May mas defensible choice kaysa none, which is dry rot.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Tanong ba ay humihingi ng named defect/problem or cause organism?
2.	Among the choices, the most defect-like answer is dry rot
3.	Fungi is too cause-based
4.	Deterioration is too vague
Common trap
Pinaka-trap si fungi dahil scientifically, sila nga ang cause.
Pero board-review items minsan gusto ang named wood condition, not the microscopic cause.
Important note
Technically, dry rot is itself a kind of decay caused by fungi, so the item is not cleanly written. Pero best answer among the choices is dry rot.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag magulo ang item, piliin ang best-fit reviewer answer, at dito iyon ay dry rot.

question: Lumber measure equivalent to 144 square inches of 1” thick lumber is:

Correct answer: a. board feet
Ulitin natin: board feet.
Mas tama sana sa strict singular usage ang board foot, pero since choice ang board feet, iyan ang tatanggapin natin.
Bakit ito ang tama?
A board foot is the volume of wood equal to:
•	1 inch thick
•	12 inches wide
•	12 inches long
That is:
1 × 12 × 12 = 144 cubic inches, or as reviewers often phrase it, 144 square inches of 1-inch-thick lumber.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. board inch – hindi ito ang standard lumber measure
•	c. board yard – hindi standard
•	d. board meter – hindi ito ang conventional term used here
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:

Board foot

3.	Since choice is pluralized as board feet, iyon ang pipiliin mo.
Common trap
Walang gaanong trap dito, except the singular/plural issue.
Sa exam, huwag kang ma-distract sa grammar kung malinaw naman ang technical term.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
1 board foot = 144 cubic inches = 1” × 12” × 12”.

question: Threaded metal pins or rods with one end having the head and with the external thread on the other end that accepts a mating nut (a metal block with a central threaded hole) to receive a bolt or other threaded material) and is used for holding together or securing different members or materials after being inserted through holes:
keyMemory: Kapag ang fastener ay may head, threaded shank, dumadaan sa holes, at may kasamang nut, ang sagot ay bolt.
explanation:

Ang description ay:

•	may external thread sa kabilang dulo
•	gumagamit ng nut
•	ipinapasok through holes
•	ginagamit pang-hold together ng members
Iyan ang classic definition ng bolt.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Rivets – wala itong threaded end for mating nut
•	c. Screws – threaded din, pero generally hindi sila defined by passing through holes and receiving a separate nut as the standard description here
•	d. Tekscrews – specific self-drilling screw type ito, hindi general bolt-with-nut definition
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	May nut bang binanggit? Oo
2.	Kapag may threaded shank plus mating nut, ang unang iisipin mo ay bolt
3.	Rivet? tanggal agad dahil hindi threaded-nut system
4.	Screw? puwedeng threaded, pero the presence of a separate nut is the killer clue
5.	Kaya bolt ang natitira
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay screws, kasi pareho rin namang may thread.
Pero tandaan ang distinction:
•	Screw often bites into material itself or into a prepared receiving material
•	Bolt usually passes through holes and works with a nut
ALE reasoning
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang sumasagot:
“May nut ba?”
Kapag oo, malaking chance na bolt ang sagot.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang fastener ay may head, threaded shank, dumadaan sa holes, at may kasamang nut, ang sagot ay bolt.

question: What is a round, steel bolt embedded in concrete or masonry use to hold down machinery, steel columns or beam plates and engine heads?
keyMemory: Kapag ang bolt ay ini-embed sa concrete para mag-hold down ng steel or machinery, ang sagot ay anchor bolt.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Anchor bolt
Ulitin natin: Anchor bolt.
Ito, ang pinakamatinding clue dito ay ito:
•	embedded in concrete or masonry
•	used to hold down machinery
•	steel columns
•	beam plates
Iyan ang classic function ng anchor bolt.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang anchor bolt ay bolt na ini-embed sa concrete or masonry para i-anchor o i-secure ang structural or mechanical element sa base. Typical uses:
•	column base plates
•	machinery bases
•	equipment supports
•	hold-down connections
So ang tanong ay hindi lang basta “anong bolt ito,” kundi anong bolt ang pang-embed at pang-hold-down sa concrete.
At iyon ay anchor bolt.
Bakit hindi J-bolt?
Ito ang common trap.
Ang J-bolt ay isang shape/type ng anchor bolt. Ibig sabihin, specific form lang siya.
Pero ang tanong ay humihingi ng general correct category para sa bolt embedded in concrete to hold things down. Kaya mas tama ang anchor bolt.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. J-bolt – specific subtype lang, hindi best general answer
•	c. Hook bolt – similar issue, shape/form-specific at hindi ito ang classic general term sa stem
•	d. Foundation bolt – tempting ito, pero mas accepted at mas standard broad term sa item ay anchor bolt
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Tanong ba ay general function o specific shape?
General function
2.	Embedded in concrete and used to hold down?
Anchoring ang concept.
3.	So piliin mo ang general exact term, not just a shape variant.
4.	Kaya ang natitira ay anchor bolt.
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang J-bolt, lalo na dahil maraming embedded bolts sa actual site ay J-type.
Pero ALE strategy:
Kapag general ang tanong, general answer ang piliin.
Philippine/ALE reasoning
Sa local structural and construction practice, ginagamit ang term na anchor bolts for bolts embedded in concrete bases to secure steel or equipment. Kaya ito ang expected answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang bolt ay ini-embed sa concrete para mag-hold down ng steel or machinery, ang sagot ay anchor bolt.

question: What do you call the perforated disks of metals, rubber or plastic placed under the head of a nut or bolt or at a joint to distribute pressure, prevent leakage, relieve friction or insulate incompatible materials?

fender is just a type; washer is the full correct category.

Kapag disk under bolt or nut para mag-spread ng load, ang tawag ay washer.

Common trap
Ang trap dito ay stove bolts, kasi strong fastener din naman iyon.
Pero tandaan: ang tanong ay hindi heavy bolted connection.
It’s asking for an alternative to metal screws in a light metal fastening context.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa light metal fastening, especially as alternative to screws, blind rivet ang strong answer.

question: For water tight connections, best to use for gutter joints
keyMemory: Para sa gutter joints na kailangang watertight, ang strongest answer ay sealant, not just a fastener.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Structural sealant
Ulitin natin: Structural sealant.
Ito, ang killer clue ay water tight connections at gutter joints.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang gutter joints kailangan:
•	hindi tumagas
•	may sealing continuity
•	makapag-accommodate ng konting movement
Sa choices, ang structural sealant ang may sealing function.
Blind rivets, nails, at bolts can hold parts together, pero sila lang by themselves ay hindi ang best answer for watertightness.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Blind rivets – pang-fasten ito, pero hindi sila ang pangunahing element para gawing watertight ang joint
•	c. Nail – hindi ito ang gutter-joint waterproof answer
•	d. Bolts – pang-connection lang, hindi pang-seal
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Ang main issue ba ay holding or sealing?
2.	Dito, malinaw na watertightness ang issue
3.	Kaya huwag pumili ng fastener lamang
4.	Piliin ang may sealing function: structural sealant
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang blind rivets, kasi ginagamit nga naman sa sheet metal joints.
Pero tandaan:
Fastening is not the same as sealing.
ALE reasoning
Kapag sinabi ng tanong na best for watertight connection, hanapin mo agad ang sealant answer kung meron.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Para sa gutter joints na kailangang watertight, ang strongest answer ay sealant, not just a fastener.

question: Bolt with a round unslotted head with a square collar beneath the head is called as:
keyMemory: Round head + square collar beneath = carriage bolt.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. carriage bolt
Ulitin natin: carriage bolt.
Ito, classic bolt identification item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang carriage bolt ay may:
•	round head
•	usually smooth, unslotted top
•	square neck/collar beneath the head
Ang square collar ang pumipigil sa pag-ikot ng bolt habang hinihigpitan ang nut.
Iyan ang signature clue.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. machine bolt – general machine/assembly bolt ito, hindi ito ang classic round-head-with-square-collar identity
•	c. molly bolt – hollow-wall anchor ito, completely different use
•	d. stove bolt – small bolt, often slotted-headed, not the round unslotted carriage type
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Key clue: round unslotted head
2.	Killer clue: square collar beneath the head
3.	Kapag may square neck/collar sa ilalim ng round head, answer is carriage bolt
4.	Molly bolt? hollow wall anchor iyon, tanggal agad
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay machine bolt dahil “bolt” din naman at pang-assembly din.
Pero the moment you see square collar beneath the head, isipin mo agad carriage bolt.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Round head + square collar beneath = carriage bolt.

•	b. Aluminum – mas mahal than ordinary steel in many cases, pero hindi pa rin ito ang pinakamahal sa choices
•	c. Iron – hindi ito ang premium roofing answer dito
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Tanong ba ay tungkol sa performance or cost?
Cost
2.	Among common metals, alin ang pinaka-premium ang dating?
Copper
3.	Steel and iron are easier to eliminate
4.	Between aluminum and copper, copper ang mas high-end at mas mahal
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay aluminum, kasi alam ng marami na mas mahal siya kaysa ordinary GI or steel.
Pero tandaan: sa listahang ito, copper pa rin ang top premium answer.
Philippine context
Sa local practice, copper roofing is not the ordinary default roofing material. Kapag copper ang gamit, usually mas specialized or premium project iyan.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa common roofing metals, ang pinakamahal sa choices ay copper.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: A tough, transparent thermoplastic characterized by its high-impact strength and used for lighting fixtures, safety glazing, and hardware.
keyMemory: Transparent + high-impact thermoplastic = polycarbonate.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Polycarbonate
Ulitin natin: Polycarbonate.
Ito, very classic plastic-material identification item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang polycarbonate ay kilala sa:
•	pagiging transparent
•	pagiging tough
•	mataas na impact strength
•	gamit sa safety glazing
•	gamit sa lighting covers and fixtures
So kung ang clue ay high impact plus transparent thermoplastic, very strong answer ang polycarbonate.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Laminated plastic – masyadong generic/product description ito, hindi exact material identity
•	b. Plastic – sobrang broad. Hindi puwedeng ito ang sagot kung may exact plastic type sa choices
•	c. Vitreous – glass-like term ito, hindi specific thermoplastic answer dito
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Hanapin ang clue na transparent thermoplastic
2.	Tapos hanapin ang mas specific clue: high-impact strength
3.	Kapag may exact material choice at may broad general choice, piliin mo ang exact material
4.	Kaya polycarbonate ang natitira
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay plastic dahil technically plastic nga naman siya.
Pero ALE trick ito:
Kapag may broad answer at may exact answer, piliin ang exact answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Transparent + high-impact thermoplastic = polycarbonate.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: What do you call a thermoplastic material that combines the transparency of glass though not scratch-proof used principally for skylights, sky domes, safety glass, and expands when hit by sun rays?
keyMemory: Acrylics ang strong answer kapag ang clue ay glass-like clarity at skylight/sky dome use.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Acrylics
Ulitin natin: Acrylics.
Ito, magandang pair ito with the previous question.
Kanina, polycarbonate was the answer dahil sa high impact strength.
Ngayon, ibang clue naman ang dapat mong kapitán.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang acrylic ay:
•	very clear, parang glass
•	common sa skylights and domes
•	not fully scratch-proof
•	thermoplastic
•	may expansion with heat/sun exposure
So the wording na:
•	transparency of glass
•	used for skylights and sky domes
•	not scratch-proof
iyan ang mas malakas na association sa acrylics.
Bakit hindi polycarbonate?
Ito ang common trap.
Polycarbonate is also transparent and ginagamit din sa glazing applications.
Pero sa reviewer logic:
•	Polycarbonate = tougher, more impact-resistant
•	Acrylic = clearer, more glass-like, common sa domes/skylights, not scratch-proof
So dahil hindi high-impact ang focus ng item, lumilipat tayo sa acrylics.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Polyethylene – hindi ito ang classic skylight glazing answer
•	c. Polystyrene – hindi rin ito ang usual answer for this architectural glazing use
•	d. Polycarbonate – possible in real life for some uses, but not the best reviewer answer here
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Compare mo agad ito sa previous item
2.	If high impact ang clue → polycarbonate
3.	If glass-like clarity, domes/skylights, not scratch-proof → acrylics
4.	Kaya acrylics ang strongest answer
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang polycarbonate dahil nauna siya sa previous question.
Board exam strategy:
Huwag magdala ng previous answer kung iba na ang clue ng current item.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Acrylics ang strong answer kapag ang clue ay glass-like clarity at skylight/sky dome use.

question: This is used along the edge and bottom of doors to provide a tight seal against water and air infiltration:
keyMemory: Kapag ang tanong ay tungkol sa tight seal against air and water infiltration around doors, ang strongest answer ay weatherstripping.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Weatherstripping
Ulitin natin: Weatherstripping.
Ito, very practical architectural detail question ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang weatherstripping ang general material/system na ginagamit sa paligid ng door edges and meeting surfaces para:
•	mabawasan ang air infiltration
•	mabawasan ang water penetration
•	magsara nang mas mahigpit ang door assembly
Importanteng clue dito ang phrase na:
•	along the edge and bottom of doors
•	tight seal
•	water and air infiltration
Iyan ang exact function ng weatherstripping.
Bakit hindi thresholds?
Magandang trap ito.
Ang threshold ay nasa ibaba ng doorway, yes, pero hindi ito ang general sealing material along the edges and bottom.
Bottom lang ang sakop niya, hindi entire sealing function around the door.
Bakit hindi door bottoms?
Another trap din ito.
Ang door bottom refers more specifically to the component attached sa ilalim ng pinto.
Pero ang tanong ay along the edge and bottom, so mas broad/general sealing system ang hinahanap.
Kaya weatherstripping ang mas tamang sagot.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Thresholds – bottom element only
•	c. Door bottoms – partial/specific bottom component lang
•	d. Stoppers – pang-control ng door swing, not air/water sealing
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Tanong ba ay sealing system o isang piraso lang sa pinto?
2.	Ang clue ay edge and bottom plus air and water infiltration
3.	So hanapin mo ang broad sealing term: weatherstripping
4.	Threshold and door bottom are too narrow
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang door bottoms kasi nabanggit ang bottom ng door.
Pero ulitin natin:
•	door bottom = one specific part
•	weatherstripping = broader sealing system around edges and bottom
Philippine practice
Sa actual detailing, puwedeng sabay gamitin ang threshold, door bottom, at weatherstripping depending on the assembly. Pero sa tanong na ito, ang general answer ay weatherstripping.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang tanong ay tungkol sa tight seal against air and water infiltration around doors, ang strongest answer ay weatherstripping.

question: Mortar or concrete which does not allow or permit the flow or passage of water through its pores or veins is considered:
keyMemory: Impermeable means hindi pinapadaan ang tubig through pores or veins.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Impermeable
Ulitin natin: Impermeable.
Ito, importante ang wording ng tanong. Ang tanong ay hindi “anong treatment ang ginawa,” kundi anong property ng material.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang ibig sabihin ng impermeable ay hindi pinapadaan ang tubig sa pores or passages.
Iyan mismo ang sinasabi ng stem:
does not allow or permit the flow or passage of water
So property term ito, hindi process term.
Bakit hindi waterproofed?
Ito ang common trap.
Ang waterproofed usually implies na may treatment or condition na ginawa para maging water-resistant.
Pero ang tanong ay mas direct sa behavior/property ng material. Kaya mas exact ang impermeable.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Waterproofed – treatment/result term, but not the best exact property word here
•	c. Integrated – hindi ito property of water passage
•	d. Pervious – kabaligtaran pa nga ito; ibig sabihin pinapadaan ang water
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Property ba ito o treatment?
Property
2.	Kung ang tanong ay “hindi pinapadaan ang tubig,” ang technical opposite ng pervious ay impermeable
3.	Kaya agad matatanggal si pervious
4.	Tapos between waterproofed and impermeable, piliin mo ang mas eksaktong property term
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay waterproofed dahil parang tama rin pakinggan.
Pero ALE trick ito:
Kapag may mas technical at mas exact term, iyon ang pipiliin.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Impermeable means hindi pinapadaan ang tubig through pores or veins.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: A type of waterproofing material that is applied to concrete or masonry surfaces that becomes homogeneous with the said surface?
keyMemory: Kapag ang waterproofing ay for concrete or masonry at parang nagiging one with the surface, strongest answer is cementitious.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Cementitious
Ulitin natin: Cementitious.
Ito, ang killer clue ay iyong phrase na “becomes homogeneous with the said surface.”
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang cementitious waterproofing ang pinaka-malapit sa concrete or masonry substrate dahil cement-based siya.
Kapag in-apply mo siya sa concrete or masonry surface, parang nagiging isang system siya with the substrate, kaya reviewer-wise, ito ang tinutukoy na homogeneous with the surface. Modern cementitious waterproofing products are also commonly described as cement-based coatings for concrete and cement render, often supplied as two-component systems. (Bostik)
Bakit hindi elastomeric?
Magandang trap ito.
Ang elastomeric is a coating system, flexible siya, yes. Pero hindi iyan ang strongest answer sa clue na homogeneous with concrete or masonry.
Bakit hindi membrane?
Ang membrane usually forms a separate layer or sheet/barrier.
Ibig sabihin, hindi iyan ang best answer kung ang emphasis ay nagiging one with the surface.
Bakit hindi asphalt?
Bituminous/asphalt systems are also surface-applied, pero hindi sila ang pinakabagay sa wording na “becomes homogeneous with the surface.”
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Concrete or masonry ba ang substrate? Oo.
2.	Alin sa choices ang pinaka-compatible at pinaka-“same family” with that substrate?
Cementitious
3.	Membrane forms separate layer, so tanggal
4.	Elastomeric and asphalt are coatings, but less exact than cementitious for this clue
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang membrane dahil waterproofing din naman iyon.
Pero tandaan:
•	membrane = hiwalay na barrier layer
•	cementitious = mas integrated ang dating with concrete/masonry surface
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang waterproofing ay for concrete or masonry at parang nagiging one with the surface, strongest answer is cementitious.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: What are the main components of cementitious waterproofing?
keyMemory: Cementitious waterproofing = usually Liquid + Powder, not resin and hardener.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Liquid and Powder
Ulitin natin: Liquid and Powder.
Ito, dapat marunong kang mag-separate ng generic product labeling versus actual component form.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang cementitious waterproofing is commonly supplied as a two-component system composed of:
•	a liquid polymer/additive component
•	a powder cement-based component (Bostik)
So kahit minsan tinatawag silang Part A and Part B, ang mas tamang sagot sa tanong na main components ay Liquid and Powder.
Bakit hindi Part A and Part B?
Ito ang common trap.
Technically, puwede silang tawaging Part A at Part B sa packaging.
Pero iyan ay labeling convention, hindi pinaka-descriptive answer ng actual material components.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Resin and Hardener – mas bagay ito sa epoxy-type systems
•	c. Cement and Asphalt – hindi ito standard cementitious waterproofing pairing
•	d. Part A and Part B – too generic compared with the actual material form
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Tanong ba ay tungkol sa actual material components or label names?
Actual components
2.	Resin and hardener? epoxy iyon, tanggal
3.	Cement and asphalt? mali pairing
4.	Between liquid+powder and Part A+Part B, piliin ang mas descriptive
5.	Kaya Liquid and Powder
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang Part A and Part B.
Board trick iyan. Puwedeng totoo sa packaging, pero hindi iyan ang best conceptual answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Cementitious waterproofing = usually Liquid + Powder, not resin and hardener.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Common waterproofing for roof decks?
keyMemory: Sa reviewer/local-practice logic, roof deck waterproofing = 4.5mm Polybond ang strongest answer.
explanation:
Best answer: d. 4.5mm Polybond
Ulitin natin: 4.5mm Polybond.
Ito, gusto kong maging maingat kasi minsan may reviewer sets na nagkakaiba-iba sa ganitong product-memory items. Pero sa stronger reviewer/local-practice logic, 4.5mm Polybond ang pinakamalakas na sagot. Some reviewer sources explicitly pair roof deck waterproofing with 4.5 mm polybond, and modified bituminous Polybond membranes are used for waterproofing roof structures. (College Sidekick)
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kapag roof deck ang usapan, mas common ang membrane waterproofing system na mas robust and thicker kaysa simple paint-type coating or integral admixture lang.
Kaya sa choices:
•	Sahara is more of integral waterproofing compound/admixture
•	Elastomeric can be coating, but not the strongest answer for classic roof deck waterproofing in this reviewer framing
•	4.5mm Polybond is the more heavy-duty membrane-type answer
Bakit hindi 3.0 kg Polybond?
Ito ang common trap.
Dahil pareho silang membrane family, pero sa roof deck application, mas malakas ang association ng 4.5mm.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Elastomeric – useful as coating, but not the best classic roof-deck waterproofing answer here
•	b. Sahara – integral waterproofing compound, not the strongest roof deck membrane answer
•	c. 3.0 kg Polybond – possible other uses, but weaker than 4.5mm for roof deck
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Roof deck ba? So likely membrane, not just admixture
2.	Tanggal si Sahara
3.	Between elastomeric and Polybond, classic heavy-duty waterproofing for roof decks favors Polybond
4.	Between 3.0 and 4.5 mm, roof deck points stronger to 4.5 mm
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang Elastomeric, dahil sikat na term iyan sa waterproofing.
Pero tandaan: hindi lahat ng waterproof coating ang strongest answer for roof deck waterproofing system.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer/local-practice logic, roof deck waterproofing = 4.5mm Polybond ang strongest answer.

question: What area of the room needs waterproofing if it’s in the basement and directly below of a living room.

choices.text: No need to waterproof the room

keyMemory: Sa basement, ang usual waterproofing concern ay walls and floor, hindi ceiling.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Floor and wall
Ulitin natin: Floor and wall.
Ito, practical building logic ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kapag basement ang room, ang primary water exposure ay usually galing sa:
•	surrounding soil
•	groundwater pressure
•	lateral moisture through basement walls
•	upward or slab-level moisture through the floor
Kaya ang pinaka-critical i-waterproof ay:
•	walls
•	floor/slab
Bakit hindi ceiling?
Kasi ang ceiling ay nasa ilalim ng living room, hindi iyon ang primary source ng groundwater infiltration sa basement condition.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Wall and Ceiling – mali ang ceiling
•	c. Ceiling and Floor – mali pa rin dahil kulang ang wall
•	d. Wall only – kulang; pati floor dapat
•	e. No need – obvious na mali
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Basement ba?
Oo.
2.	Saan kadalasang pumapasok ang tubig sa basement?
Walls at floor
3.	So automatic tanggal ang choices na may ceiling.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay isipin na dahil may room above, baka ceiling ang iisipin.
Pero sa basement waterproofing, soil-contact surfaces ang bantayan mo.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa basement, ang usual waterproofing concern ay walls and floor, hindi ceiling.

question: SikaProof Bentonite is what type of water proofing?

Ang bentonite ay clay material na kilala sa kakayahang:

Ang trap dito ay membrane, kasi maraming bentonite systems sheet-like rin.

Ito, halos reinforcement lang ito ng Question 183.

Dahil Thoroseal ang classic reviewer answer para sa:

question: Contains a framework consisting of stiles and rails which bordered the expanded honeycombed core of corrugated fiberboard or of a grid of interlocking horizontal and vertical wood strips:
keyMemory: Kapag ang clue ay honeycomb core o cellular lightweight interior core, ang sagot ay Hollow Core Flush Door.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Hollow Core Flush Door
Ulitin natin: Hollow Core Flush Door.
Ito, descriptive door-construction item ito. Ang pinakamalaking clue rito ay ang phrase na:
•	stiles and rails
•	honeycombed core
•	corrugated fiberboard
•	grid of interlocking wood strips
Iyan ang classic description ng hollow core flush door.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang hollow core flush door ay hindi solid sa loob. May frame siya sa paligid, tapos ang interior core niya ay madalas:
•	honeycomb
•	cellular core
•	lightweight grid structure
Ginagawa ito para:
•	gumaan ang door
•	bumaba ang cost
•	maging practical for interior use
So kapag nakita mo ang salitang honeycombed core, halos sinasabi na agad ng tanong ang sagot.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Solid Core Flush Door – mali dahil ang solid core ay may mas dense, mas solid na laman tulad ng bonded blocks, particle core, o mineral core. Hindi iyan honeycomb.
•	c. Wood Framed Flush Door – ito ang common trap.
May stiles and rails din siya, oo, pero ang defining clue dito ay honeycombed core of corrugated fiberboard. Mas direct iyon sa hollow core flush door.
•	d. Wood Panel Door – ibang family ito. May visible panels ito, samantalang ang flush door ay smooth-faced.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Flush door ba o panel door?
Dahil sa flush smooth-face construction description, tanggal agad ang wood panel door.
2.	Solid ba o hollow?
Dahil sa honeycomb/core grid clue, tanggal si solid core.
3.	Between hollow core at wood framed flush door, alin ang pinaka-defined by honeycomb/core grid?
Hollow Core Flush Door
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay wood framed flush door, kasi may stiles and rails din naman iyon.
Pero tandaan mo ito:
•	wood framed flush door = frame plus wood face construction as a general type
•	hollow core flush door = may lightweight honeycomb/cellular core, at iyan ang unique clue dito
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang clue ay honeycomb core o cellular lightweight interior core, ang sagot ay Hollow Core Flush Door.

question: Which of the ff is not included in specifying Fire Rated doors?
keyMemory: Sa reviewer item na ito, ang hindi included ay Handling. Pero sa real documentation, bantayan mo ang difference ng handling at handing.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Handling
Ulitin natin: Handling.
Ito, kailangan mong tingnan kung alin ang specification item at alin ang hindi natural kasama sa door specification.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Kapag nagsi-specify ka ng fire-rated door, ang normal mong isasama ay:
•	dimension
•	fire rating
•	fire type or door type/classification
Ang handling ay hindi standard core item sa fire-rated door specification sa level ng choices na ito.
Bakit hindi dimension?

Bakit hindi fire rating?
Ito nga ang pinakaimportanteng item sa fire-rated door. Ito ang nagsasabi kung 20 min, 45 min, 1 hour, at iba pa.
Bakit hindi fire type?
Kasama rin ito sa classification or type identification ng fire door assembly.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Dimension – essential specification item

Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:

o	Dimension? yes, kailangan
o	Fire rating? definitely yes

o	Handling? hindi ito ang usual core spec item

Ang trap dito ay kung iisipin mo na baka “handling” means handing or handing of door, like left hand/right hand.
At oo, sa door schedules, may term na handing sa actual architectural documentation.
Pero tandaan: ang nakasulat dito ay handling, at sa reviewer phrasing na ito, siya ang intended outlier.
Importanteng maging honest tayo dito:

Pero dahil handling ang nakalagay, and among these choices, iyon ang likely intended incorrect choice.

Sa actual door schedule and door specifications, puwedeng kasama ang:
•	size/dimensions
•	fire rating

•	frame
•	hardware
•	handing
Pero sa printed reviewer wording, handling ang pinakamalamang na intended wrong choice.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer item na ito, ang hindi included ay Handling.
Pero sa real documentation, bantayan mo ang difference ng handling at handing.

•	screw
Ito ang practical at neat na method para sa door/frame installation. Hindi mo kailangan ng sobrang heavy-duty bolt kung jamb lang ang ini-mount mo.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. concrete nail – puwede sa ibang direct fastening situations, pero hindi ito ang pinaka-standard at pinaka-controlled method for aluminum jamb installation.
•	c. Tek screw – ang tek screw ay mas pang-sheet metal to metal, or roofing/light metal fastening. Hindi ito ang best answer para sa concrete or masonry substrate.
•	d. expansion bolt – ito ang common trap.
Kasi parang “mas matibay.” Oo, pero mas pang-heavy anchorage ito. For aluminum jambs, mas typical ang screw with expansion plug.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Material ba ay aluminum jamb?
Oo, light architectural frame.
2.	Substrate ba ay concrete or masonry?
Oo.
3.	So ang practical solution ay drilled hole + plug + screw.
4.	Tanggal ang tek screw dahil mali ang substrate.
5.	Tanggal ang expansion bolt dahil too heavy for the usual jamb-fixing context.
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang expansion bolt dahil mas “engineer” pakinggan.
Pero tandaan:
Hindi dahil mas mabigat o mas heavy-duty ang fastener, iyon na agad ang tama.
Dapat sakto sa application.
Philippine practice
Sa actual local installation, common ang screws with plugs or anchors for door jamb/frame installation into masonry or concrete openings.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag aluminum jamb to concrete/masonry, strongest practical answer is screw with expansion plug.

question: Who is the highly skilled worker trained to install aluminum jambs to concrete?
keyMemory: Kapag specialized aluminum frame/jamb system, ang best answer sa installer ay supplier installer.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Supplier installer
Ulitin natin: supplier installer.
Ito, very practical construction-procurement question ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa aluminum doors, frames, and jamb systems, madalas ang pinaka-trained at pinaka-specialized na gumagawa ng installation ay iyong supplier installer or installer coming from the specialized supplier/fabricator.
Bakit? Kasi sila ang:
•	may alam sa exact product system
•	may tamang accessories and anchorage details
•	may familiarity sa frame tolerances and installation sequence
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Finishing mason – masonry finish expert ito, pero hindi siya ang pinaka-specialized aluminum-jamb installer
•	b. Finishing carpenter – wood/joinery/finish carpentry ang mas natural na scope nito
•	c. Finishing steelman – structural/light steel related ang dating nito, pero hindi ito ang classic answer sa architectural aluminum jamb installation
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Specialized proprietary product ba ito?
Yes, aluminum jamb/frame system
2.	Kapag specialized product, sino kadalasang may exact installation know-how?
Supplier installer
3.	Tanggal ang trade workers na hindi iyon ang primary specialization
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay finishing carpenter, dahil pinto/jamb nga naman ang usapan.
Pero huwag mong kalimutan na aluminum jamb ito, hindi wood jamb.
Philippine practice
Sa local projects, very common na ang aluminum or glazing-related systems ay may kasamang specialized supplier installation.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag specialized aluminum frame/jamb system, ang best answer sa installer ay supplier installer.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: What is the system of mass production of cabinets and furniture gaining popularity and acceptance in the Philippines who are conscious of time and quality?
keyMemory: Sa cabinets and furniture, ang practical standardized system is modular system.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. modular system
Ulitin natin: modular system.
Ito, practical furniture/interior fit-out question ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang modular system ang tumutukoy sa standardized, pre-planned, repeatable components for:
•	cabinets
•	kitchen systems
•	storage units
•	furniture systems
Gaining popularity ito dahil:
•	mas mabilis ang installation
•	mas controlled ang quality
•	standardized ang production
•	mas madaling i-coordinate at i-fabricate
Bakit hindi mass production?
Ito ang common trap.
Ang mass production ay general manufacturing concept lang.
Ang tanong ay humihingi ng specific system used in cabinets and furniture. Mas exact ang modular system.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. conventional system – mas traditional/custom build ito, hindi ito ang system being highlighted
•	c. “bakyawan” – not the proper technical answer here
•	d. mass production – too broad, not the exact furniture/cabinet system term
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Ang tanong ba ay broad manufacturing concept or specific cabinetry/furniture system?
Specific system
2.	Kapag standardized cabinet/furniture components ang usapan, ang term ay modular
3.	Tanggal ang broad answer na mass production
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang mass production, kasi nasa stem na nga ang phrase na iyon.
Board trick iyan.
Minsan ginagamit ang broad phrase sa tanong, pero ang sagot ay specific system term.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa cabinets and furniture, ang practical standardized system is modular system.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: What are the functions of a window?
keyMemory: Ang window ay may multiple functions, so dito ang sagot ay all of the above.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. all of the above
Ulitin natin: all of the above.
Ito, napakaganda nito dahil mismong page may note na five functions of windows, at kasama nga rito ang:
•	ventilation
•	vision
•	escape
at iba pa.
Bakit ito ang tama?
A window can function for:
•	ventilation – para sa hangin
•	vision – para makakita papasok at palabas
•	escape – in some emergency conditions
Since lahat ng listed choices ay valid functions, ang tamang sagot ay all of the above.
Bakit hindi isa lang sa tatlo?
Kasi ang tanong ay plural:
“functions”
Kapag lahat ng listed items ay valid, huwag kang mamili ng isa lang.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	I-check isa-isa:
o	ventilation? yes
o	escape? yes
o	vision? yes
2.	Since valid silang lahat, answer is all of the above
Common trap
Minsan napipili lang ng examinee ang ventilation dahil iyon ang pinaka-common association ng window.
Pero huwag kalimutan ang ibang functions.
Philippine practice
Sa architecture, windows are not just for air and light. They also have visual, safety, and architectural roles.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Ang window ay may multiple functions, so dito ang sagot ay all of the above.

question: During the Design Stage of the house of your friend asks you to put a window on the second floor which projects from the main wall of the house? What will you do?

choices.text: Tell your friend it’s not possible

keyMemory: Projecting upper-floor window = oriel window.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Introduce an oriel window
Ulitin natin: oriel window.
Ito, very classic architectural terminology item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang oriel window ay isang window that projects from the wall of the building, commonly on upper floors.
Iyan mismo ang clue ng tanong:
•	second floor
•	projects from the main wall
So exact architectural term ang hinahanap, at iyon ay oriel window.
Bakit hindi enclosed balcony?
Ito ang common trap.
An enclosed balcony also projects, yes, but it is not simply the proper term for a projecting window.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Introduce an enclosed balcony – mas malaki at ibang spatial element ito
•	b. Just put a window – too vague, not the technical answer
•	c. Tell your friend it’s not possible – obvious na mali dahil possible naman
•	d. Introduce an oriel window – exact term
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Upper-floor projecting opening ba ang hinahanap?
Oo.
2.	Hanap ka ng exact architectural term, hindi generic object
3.	Sa choices, ang only technical correct term ay oriel window
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang enclosed balcony dahil parehong naka-project.
Pero tandaan:
•	balcony = accessible space
•	oriel window = projecting window form
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Projecting upper-floor window = oriel window.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Window opening for tropical climates to prevent rain from entering a room even while the window is open:
keyMemory: Para sa tropical climate at open-window rain protection, ang sagot ay awning window.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Awning
Ulitin natin: Awning.
Ito, climate-responsive window question ito, very practical sa Philippine setting.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang awning window ay hinged at the top and opens outward at the bottom, creating a protective outward sloping sash.
Dahil dito:
•	puwedeng pumasok ang hangin
•	nababawasan ang pasok ng ulan
•	bagay sa tropical climates with frequent rain
Bakit hindi casement?
Ang casement window opens like a door. Maganda ang ventilation, pero hindi ito ang pinaka-rain-protective answer while open.
Bakit hindi hopper?
Hopper usually opens inward or downward depending on configuration, at hindi ito ang classic tropical-rain answer.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Casement – good ventilation, but not the strongest rain-protection choice here
•	b. Hopper – not the usual answer for keeping rain out while open
•	c. Sliding – opening remains exposed to wind-driven rain
•	d. Awning – best answer
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Key clue: prevent rain from entering even while open
2.	Hanapin ang window type na parang may maliit na built-in cover effect kapag naka-open
3.	Iyan ang awning
4.	Sliding and casement are easier to eliminate once you think about rain direction
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay casement, dahil common choice din ito sa tropical design.
Pero kapag ang clue ay open kahit umuulan, mas lamang ang awning.
Philippine context
Sa tropical and rainy climate tulad sa Pilipinas, ang awning window is a very sensible answer for ventilation with rain protection.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Para sa tropical climate at open-window rain protection, ang sagot ay awning window.

question: A window which projects outside the main line of a building and the compartment in which it is located extends to the floor is known as
keyMemory: Bay window ang sagot kapag ang projecting window space ay umaabot sa floor.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Bay window
Ulitin natin: Bay window.
Ito, very important ang distinction between bay window at oriel window. Madalas iyan ang trap.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang bay window ay window projection na:
•	lumalabas sa main wall line
•	at ang recessed/projection space niya extends to the floor
Ibig sabihin, parang may maliit na projecting alcove or extension ang room.
Bakit hindi oriel window?
Ito ang common trap.
Ang oriel window ay projecting window din, pero kadalasan:
•	nasa upper floor
•	naka-project from wall
•	hindi umaabot hanggang ground or floor line in the same projecting base sense
So kapag sinabing the compartment extends to the floor, mas bay window ang tamang sagot.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Hopper window – opening type ito, hindi projecting window form
•	c. Oriel window – projecting din, pero not the best answer dahil sa floor-extension clue
•	d. Cantilevered window – descriptive lang ang dating, hindi ito ang classic architectural term na hinahanap dito
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Projecting ba ang clue? Oo.
2.	May room compartment bang kasamang lumalabas at umaabot sa floor? Oo.
3.	Kaya bay window, hindi hopper.
4.	Between bay at oriel, tandaan:
o	bay = extends to floor
o	oriel = upper projecting window type
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang oriel window kasi parehong projecting window.
Pero ang killer clue ay extends to the floor.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Bay window ang sagot kapag ang projecting window space ay umaabot sa floor.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: What window is the easiest to clean?
keyMemory: Sa reviewer logic, sliding window ang easiest to clean.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Sliding
Ulitin natin: Sliding.
Ito, sinagot na rin tayo ng note sa page:
Ang sliding window may sash that is usually removable for easy cleaning.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang sliding window ang easiest to clean sa reviewer logic dahil:
•	may removable sash
•	madali ma-access
•	mas practical linisin kaysa windows with many blades or moving slats
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Casement – okay linisin in some situations, pero hindi ito ang strongest reviewer answer
•	b. Jalousie – maraming blades/louvers, kaya mas matrabaho linisin
•	d. Fixed – kahit walang moving parts, hindi ito ang expected answer here because access is not always easiest, especially depending on location
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Which one has the easiest removable component?
Sliding
2.	Jalousie? alisin agad dahil maraming piraso
3.	Casement? okay, but not as strong as sliding in this reviewer note
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang fixed, kasi iisipin mo “wala namang moving parts.”
Pero practical cleaning access-wise, sliding with removable sash ang mas accepted answer dito.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer logic, sliding window ang easiest to clean.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: Either a fixed or movable part of the window that holds the pane of glass or other materials:
keyMemory: The real key term here is sash. Sa choices given, the best fit is b. sash and glazing.
explanation:
Best answer: b. sash and glazing
Ulitin natin: best answer, sash and glazing.
Aaminin ko, awkward ang choice set. Ang tamang technical word sana rito is really sash, kasi iyon ang part that holds the glass. Pero dahil wala ang “sash” alone sa choices, ang best available answer ay b. sash and glazing.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
Ang sash ang bahagi ng window na humahawak sa glass or panel infill. Since choice b is the only one containing the key term sash, iyon ang intended answer.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. frame – frame is the outer enclosure/support, not the specific member holding the glass panel in the opening the way a sash does
•	c. mullions – vertical dividers ito between window units/openings
•	d. hopper muntin – mali or awkward term combination lang ito in this context
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Hanapin mo agad ang known window term na humahawak ng glass
Sash
2.	Wala si “sash” alone, so hanapin ang choice na may sash
3.	Kaya b ang best answer
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay frame, kasi iniisip ng marami buong window framing na agad.
Pero tandaan:
•	frame = outer supporting frame
•	sash = holder of the glass/panel
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
The real key term here is sash. Sa choices given, the best fit is b. sash and glazing.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: What do you use as steel window section for muntin?
keyMemory: Sa steel window muntins, the expected reviewer answer is T-bar.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. T bar
Ulitin natin: T bar.
Ito, terminology/detailing item ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang muntin is the small dividing member between panes. Sa steel window sections, ang T-bar is the most fitting section because its profile works well as a divider/member between glazing areas.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Z bar – ginagamit sa ibang framing conditions, not the classic muntin answer
•	b. flat bar – too plain and not the best standard sectional answer
•	c. I bar – hindi ito ang practical muntin section answer dito
•	d. T bar – strongest answer
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Muntin = small divider
2.	Hanapin ang profile na pinaka-sensible as glazing divider
T bar
3.	Flat bar is too generic; Z and I are less fitting for the intended use
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay flat bar, kasi simple at parang puwede naman.
Pero sa reviewer/detailing logic, T-bar ang mas standard answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa steel window muntins, the expected reviewer answer is T-bar.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: A manufactured assembly of a frame, sash, glazing and necessary hardware, made to fit a window opening
keyMemory: Kapag complete preassembled window package, sagot ay window unit.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Window Unit
Ulitin natin: Window Unit.
Ito, ang clue ay manufactured assembly ng maraming parts together.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang window unit is the complete assembled package including:
•	frame
•	sash
•	glazing
•	hardware
na ginawa para i-fit sa opening.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Window Assembly – tunog plausible, pero window unit ang mas standard specific term sa reviewer sense
•	c. Window Frame – frame lang ito, kulang
•	d. Window Sash – sash lang din ito, kulang
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Complete package ba ito? Oo.
2.	Kung complete package, huwag pumili ng part lang
3.	Frame and sash, tanggal agad
4.	Between assembly and unit, mas accepted reviewer term ang window unit
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay window assembly kasi halos same thought.
Pero sa printed reviewer logic, ang mas standard object term is window unit.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag complete preassembled window package, sagot ay window unit.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: These are devices for windows not part of the standard supply of the window package used to operate its opening and closing:

explanation:

Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang operators ang general term for devices used to open and close windows or doors when they are not part of the standard basic package.

•	b. Motion Sensors – not the general term for opening/closing mechanism

Ang trap dito ay window closer dahil opening/closing function din naman.
Pero masyadong specific iyon.

Operators ang umbrella term for these special opening/closing devices.

question: An opening in the roof for admitting light is called

Ito, direct architectural term item ito.

A skylight is a roof opening glazed or designed for admitting daylight into the interior.

question: Silicone sealants are available in grades, except?
keyMemory: Sa building-sealant reviewer context, the expected except answer is Electrical Grade.
explanation:
Ulitin natin: best reviewer answer, Electrical Grade.
Gusto kong maging honest dito: sa broader industrial context, may tinatawag talagang electrical/electronic grade silicones.
Pero sa building/window-sealant reviewer logic, ang intended outlier dito ay Electrical Grade.
Bakit ito ang best reviewer answer?
Sa architectural/building sealant context, the familiar grades commonly discussed are:
•	Construction Grade
•	Glazing Grade
•	Sanitary Grade
Kaya sa reviewer framing, Electrical Grade ang hindi kabilang sa standard building-sealant grade list.
Bakit kailangan maging maingat?
Kasi in real-world broader product markets, electrical-grade silicone exists.
So this is one of those items na acceptable as reviewer answer, but not perfectly universal.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Building/window sealant context ba ang usapan? Oo.
2.	Hanapin ang outlier na mas outside building-application grading
3.	Kaya Electrical Grade ang likely intended answer
Common trap
Walang masyadong trap except the fact na puwede kang malito kung alam mo na may electrical silicone products nga.
So remember:
•	reviewer answer here = Electrical Grade
•	broader industry reality = not totally clean item
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa building-sealant reviewer context, the expected except answer is Electrical Grade.

question: Identify:
keyMemory: Sa image na ito, the strongest identification is casement window lock.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Casement window lock
Ulitin natin: Casement window lock.
Base sa image, ang hardware shown looks like window locking handles/locking pieces, not a hinge and not a long espagnolette assembly.
Bakit ito ang tama?
The picture shows small metal operating/locking pieces used for a window sash.
Hindi ito mukhang:
•	hinge leaves with knuckles
•	security slide bolt
•	long rod-type espagnolette mechanism
So the strongest answer is casement window lock.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Security bolt – usually bolt-style sliding security piece ang itsura
•	c. Window hinge – hinge would show leaf-and-pin configuration
•	d. Espagnolet lock – usually associated with rod/rod-locking system, not the simple handle/lock image shown
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Hinge ba ang itsura?
Hindi.
2.	Rod-type multi-point lock ba?
Hindi rin.
3.	Sliding bolt ba?
Hindi.
4.	Natitira ang casement window lock
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang espagnolet lock dahil lock hardware din iyon.
Pero the image is much simpler and shorter than the usual espagnolette mechanism.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa image na ito, the strongest identification is casement window lock.

question: Identify:
keyMemory: Kapag recessed pulls ang image with locking component, the answer is sliding door lock.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Sliding door lock
Ulitin natin: Sliding door lock.
Ito, iyong image shows two recessed pull plates plus a central locking piece. Iyan ang typical look ng sliding door lock set, not just simple finger pulls.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang sliding door lock often includes:
•	recessed pulls on both sides
•	central latch/lock component
Kung finger pulls lang iyan, mas simple sana at walang separate locking element.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Finger pulls – ito ang common trap.
Recessed pull shape oo, pero kulang ito sa lock element.
•	c. Flush bolt – ginagamit sa inactive leaf ng double door, hindi ito ang image
•	d. Pull hatch – hindi ito standard answer sa image na ito
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Recessed hardware ba siya? Oo.
2.	May lock/latch component ba? Oo.
3.	So hindi lang siya simpleng finger pull
4.	Therefore: sliding door lock
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang finger pulls dahil kahawig ng recessed hardware shape.
Pero tandaan:
•	finger pull = pull only
•	sliding door lock = recessed pull plus locking function
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag recessed pulls ang image with locking component, the answer is sliding door lock.

question: Identify:
keyMemory: Kapag ang locking hardware ay mahaba at recessed sa pinto, strongest answer is flush bolt.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Flush bolt
Ulitin natin: Flush bolt.
Ito, image-identification item ito. Ang nasa picture ay iyong mahaba at makitid na recessed bolt hardware na ini-install flush sa edge o face ng door leaf, usually sa inactive leaf ng double door.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang flush bolt ay:
•	pahabang hardware
•	recessed or mounted flush sa pinto
•	ginagamit para i-lock ang inactive leaf ng double door sa taas at/o baba
Iyong itsura sa image, hindi siya round-body barrel bolt na surface mounted lang. Mas mukhang built-in or recessed long plate hardware siya, kaya flush bolt ang tamang sagot.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?

Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:

Door hardware.

Oo.

question: A sound, air or light seal usually installed at the bottom of the door and goes up when the door is open and shuts when the door is close:
keyMemory: Kapag ang seal sa ilalim ng pinto ay automatic bumababa pag nakasara at umaangat pag bukas, ang sagot ay automatic door bottoms.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. automatic door bottoms
Ulitin natin: automatic door bottoms.
Ito, sobrang importanteng clue dito ay iyong action niya:
•	nasa ilalim ng pinto
•	umaangat when the door is open
•	bumababa or nagsa-seal when the door is closed
Iyan na iyan ang automatic door bottom o automatic drop seal concept.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang automatic door bottom is a hardware/seal assembly installed at the bottom edge of a door that:
•	drops down kapag sumasara ang pinto
•	nagsi-seal against sound, air, light, sometimes smoke/draft
•	umaangat ulit kapag bumukas ang pinto para hindi sumayad sa floor
So hindi lang siya simpleng weatherstrip. May automatic movement siyang involved.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. door stops – ang purpose nito ay pigilan ang pinto sa pagbukas para hindi bumangga sa wall. Hindi ito bottom seal.
•	c. flush bolt – locking hardware ito para sa inactive leaf ng double door, hindi sealing device.
•	d. door seal – trap ito.
Kasi puwede namang sabihin broadly na seal siya. Pero ang tanong ay mas specific:
goes up when open, shuts when closed.
Ibig sabihin, hindi generic seal ang hinahanap — automatic door bottoms ang exact answer.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Sealing ba ito or locking?
Sealing
2.	Automatic action ba ang clue?
Oo
3.	Tanggal agad ang flush bolt at door stop
4.	Between door seal and automatic door bottoms, piliin ang mas exact mechanical term
Common trap
Ang biggest trap dito ay door seal dahil parang tama naman.
Pero tandaan:
Kapag may specific operating behavior sa stem, hanapin ang specific hardware term, hindi broad term.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang seal sa ilalim ng pinto ay automatic bumababa pag nakasara at umaangat pag bukas, ang sagot ay automatic door bottoms.

question: Most secured type of hinge?
keyMemory: Sa choices na ito, ang most secured type of hinge is Full Mortise Butt Hinge.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Full Mortise Butt Hinge
Ulitin natin: Full Mortise Butt Hinge.
Ito, gusto kong himayin mabuti kasi may tempting na choice na concealed hinge.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa regular door construction and hardware practice, ang full mortise butt hinge ang pinaka-secure sa choices dahil:
•	naka-mortise siya sa door
•	naka-mortise din sa jamb
•	mas flush ang installation
•	mas protected ang hinge leaves kaysa surface-mounted types
•	ito rin ang standard heavy-duty/common hinge for many doors
Kaya kung ang tanong ay most secured type in the context of ordinary door hinge installation, full mortise butt hinge ang strongest answer.
Bakit hindi concealed hinge?
Ito ang common trap.
Bakit? Kasi ang word na concealed sounds more secure.
Pero isipin mo ito:
•	ang concealed hinge is more about pagiging hindi nakikita
•	hindi ibig sabihin automatic na siya ang best secure hinge for standard door construction
•	sa reviewer/door hardware logic, ang full mortise butt hinge ang mas accepted answer bilang secure installation type for doors
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Full Surface Hinge – ito ang least secure among mortising options dahil exposed at hindi naka-recess.
•	b. Butt Hinge – too general ito. May different ways of installing butt hinges. Since may mas specific version na full mortise butt hinge, iyon ang pipiliin mo.
•	c. Concealed Hinge – tempting, pero hindi ito ang best reviewer answer for this question.
•	d. Full Mortise Butt Hinge – most exact and strongest.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Surface-mounted ba o mortised?
Usually mas secure ang mortised
2.	General ba o specific?
Kapag may butt hinge at may full mortise butt hinge, piliin ang specific stronger option
3.	Concealed sounds attractive, but ask yourself:
“Ito ba ang usual secure hinge answer for regular doors?”
Reviewer logic says full mortise butt hinge
Common trap
Pinaka-trap talaga ang concealed hinge.
Pero sa board exam, huwag ka lang padadala sa dramatic sounding words like concealed, invisible, specialized.
Minsan ang tamang sagot ay iyong standard but properly installed hardware.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa choices na ito, ang most secured type of hinge is Full Mortise Butt Hinge.

question: This is the principal paint for exterior wood surfaces. It contains white lead as the white paint pigment, together with color pigments and extenders in a vehicle consisting of drying oil (usually linseed oil), dryers and the solvent or thinner (turpentine).
keyMemory: Kapag ang paint composition includes linseed oil and turpentine, think oil paint.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Oil Paint
Ulitin natin: oil paint.
Ito, halos built-in na ang answer sa stem dahil binanggit na ang composition:
•	white lead pigment
•	drying oil
•	linseed oil
•	turpentine
Classic iyan for oil paint.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang principal traditional paint for exterior wood in this reviewer framing is oil paint because of:
•	oil-based vehicle
•	good penetration and film for wood
•	classic exterior wood finishing association
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Elastomeric Paint – more for masonry/wall flexible coating systems
•	c. Enamel Paint – finish category ito, often varnish-based in older definitions, but not the direct classic composition described
•	d. none of the above – mali dahil may exact answer naman
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	May drying oil and turpentine ba sa stem? Oo.
2.	Classic oil-paint language iyan
3.	So answer = oil paint
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay enamel paint because ginagamit din sa wood in some cases.
Pero the stem is describing the classical oil paint composition, not enamel.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang paint composition includes linseed oil and turpentine, think oil paint.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: The pigments used are obtained from various earth clays after which they are dissolved in linseed oil and the coloring particles remain suspended between the oil molecules:
keyMemory: Kapag ang description ay pigments suspended in oil for coloring wood, ang sagot ay oil stains.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Oil Stains
Ulitin natin: oil stains.
Ito, finishing-material composition question ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang description ng stem ay tumutukoy sa:
•	natural earth pigments
•	carried in linseed oil
•	color particles suspended in oil
Iyan ang logic ng oil stains.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Varnish – clear/protective finish ito, not pigment-suspension color material
•	c. Shellac – resin-based finish/sealer ito, not this pigment-in-oil description
•	d. none of the above – mali dahil may tamang answer
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	May pigment ba? Oo.
2.	May linseed oil ba? Oo.
3.	Decorative coloring for wood ba ang dating? Oo.
4.	So answer = oil stains
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay varnish dahil finish-related din.
Pero tandaan:
•	varnish = mostly clear protective coat
•	stain = may coloring particles
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang description ay pigments suspended in oil for coloring wood, ang sagot ay oil stains.

question: What do you call a coating applied to a surface to have a better adhesion of the finishing coat?
keyMemory: Primer ang ginagamit para kumapit nang maayos ang finishing coat.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Primer

Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang primer ang preparatory coat na ginagamit para:
•	mapabuti ang adhesion
•	ma-seal ang substrate
•	maihanda ang surface bago ang finish coat
Bakit mali ang iba?
•	Base coat – puwedeng part ng coating system, pero hindi ito ang pinaka-general adhesion-prep term
•	Ground coat – mas specific sa certain systems
•	Neutralizer – ibang function ito

Ang clue mo rito ay better adhesion of finishing coat.
Kapag iyan ang phrase, almost automatic na primer.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay base coat.
Pero tandaan: ang tanong ay hindi about sequence lang. Ang tanong ay tungkol sa adhesion preparation.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Primer ang ginagamit para kumapit nang maayos ang finishing coat.

•	waterproofing – not the primary role
•	add gloss – clearly wrong

3.	Kaya sagot: better adhesion of epoxy paint

Pinaka-trap ang rust protection.
Pero primer questions often test whether you know the system function, not just a side benefit.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
In a coating system, the primer’s key job is adhesion.
