question: Which of the following is the best type of paint to be used for a properly primed steel?
keyMemory: For properly primed steel, the strongest answer among these choices is epoxy enamel.
explanation:
Best answer: c. Epoxy Enamel
Ulitin natin: Epoxy Enamel.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
For properly primed steel, epoxy enamel is the strongest answer among the choices because it is a tougher, more durable protective finish system for metal than latex-based paints. Epoxy enamel systems are used over epoxy primer for durable protection, while quick-drying enamel is also used on metal but is generally the less specialized option. (Boysen)
Bakit hindi Quick Dry Enamel?
Ito ang main trap.
Quick Dry Enamel is indeed used for wood and metal. Pero ang tanong ay best type for properly primed steel. That wording pushes toward the more protective, more system-oriented answer: epoxy enamel.
Bakit mali ang iba?
•	Latex – not the best for steel
•	Acrylic Latex – masonry/wall/general surface answer, weaker for steel
•	Quick Dry Enamel – usable, but weaker than epoxy enamel when the question asks for the best type
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Steel ba? Yes.
2.	Properly primed ba? Yes.
3.	So likely industrial/stronger finish ang hanap
4.	Between quick-dry enamel and epoxy enamel, choose the more durable metal-finish answer
5.	Sagot: Epoxy Enamel
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang Quick Dry Enamel, dahil common at familiar siyang pang-metal.
Pero “common” is not always “best.”
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
For properly primed steel, the strongest answer among these choices is epoxy enamel.

Elimination method

Ang trap dito ay resin, kasi liquid din naman at puwedeng coating component.
Pero ang tanong ay humihingi ng actual finish material, hindi chemical class lang.

question: A construction technique for color production and reproduction using the Munsell color principle, a color has its degree of colorfulness or intensity because of its:

keyMemory: Kapag ang process includes aggregate forced down + screed + float + steel trowel, sagot ay steel-trowelled finish.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Steel-trowelled finish
Ulitin natin: steel-trowelled finish.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Mismong note sa right side nagsabi:
•	aggregate is forced below the surface
•	leveled with straight wood screed
•	given wood float finish
•	then steel trowelled
Iyan ang sequence ng steel-trowelled finish.
Bakit hindi wood float finish?
Ito ang trap.
Oo, may wood float step sa description. Pero ang final named finish sa sequence is steel-trowelled finish.
Bakit mali ang iba?
•	b. Wood float finish – step lang ito, hindi final answer
•	c. Tooled finish – ibang finish method
•	d. Integral Colored-Cement Floor Finish – may color topping pa ito, which is not stated in the stem
Elimination method
1.	Tingnan mo kung may final finishing step beyond floating.
2.	If the process ends in steel trowelling, answer is steel-trowelled finish.
3.	Huwag huminto sa intermediate step na wood float.
Common trap
Pinaka-trap ang wood float finish dahil literal na nabanggit sa stem.
Pero tandaan: tanong is what is called, meaning final named finish, hindi isa lang sa steps.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang process includes aggregate forced down + screed + float + steel trowel, sagot ay steel-trowelled finish.

question: Type of tile taken from either natural clay or shale, similar to brick in performance, material, color and method of installation?
keyMemory: Kapag ang tile ay brick-like in material and installation, ang sagot ay brick tile.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Brick tile
Ulitin natin: brick tile.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang stem itself says:
•	natural clay or shale
•	similar to brick
•	similar in performance, material, color, and installation
So halos sinasabi na ng tanong ang sagot: brick tile.
Bakit mali ang iba?
•	marble tile – stone tile ito

•	synthetic granite tile – ibang material concept ito
Elimination method
Kapag ang stem ay sobrang lapit sa isang choice, piliin mo iyon.
Dito, brick-like in material and installation = brick tile.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay ceramic tile dahil clay product din naman.
Pero may mas exact answer na brick tile.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang tile ay brick-like in material and installation, ang sagot ay brick tile.

question: Resembling glass, as in transparency, hardness, brittleness, luster or having low or no porosity
keyMemory: Glass-like quality = vitreous.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Vitreous
Ulitin natin: vitreous.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang vitreous means glass-like or having glassy qualities:
•	hard
•	brittle
•	low porosity
•	lustrous/glassy character
Bakit mali ang iba?
•	homogeneous – uniform lang ang ibig sabihin nito
•	acrylic – plastic material ito
•	cinder – waste/byproduct aggregate term ito
Elimination method
1.	Hanapin ang term na directly means glass-like.
2.	Sagot: vitreous
Common trap
Wala masyado rito. Straight vocabulary item ito.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Glass-like quality = vitreous.

question: This consists of thoroughly bonded composition of thermoplastic binder, asbestos and other fibers, inert filler materials (various stone dust, diatomite, mica etc) and inert color pigments formed under pressure while hot and cut to size.
keyMemory: Kapag ang resilient flooring ay described as hot-pressed thermoplastic binder with fibers and mineral fillers, strongest answer is asphalt tile/sheet.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Asphalt tile/sheet
Ulitin natin: Asphalt tile/sheet.
Ito, ang pinakamalakas na clue dito ay iyong composition mismo. Hindi lang ito simpleng “anong floor finish ito,” kundi anong material ang gawa sa thermoplastic binder plus fillers and fibers.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang asphalt tile/sheet ay classic resilient flooring material na gawa sa:
•	thermoplastic binder
•	mineral fillers
•	fibers
•	pigments
•	formed under heat and pressure
Iyong phrase na thermoplastic binder plus formed under pressure while hot sobrang tumuturo sa asphalt-based flooring.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Vinyl tile/sheet – ito rin ay resilient flooring, oo, pero sa reviewer logic mas direct ito sa solid vinyl / vinyl composition family, hindi sa exact composition na binigay sa stem na may asbestos/fibers/mineral fillers in that old-school description.
•	b. Rubber tile/sheet – rubber flooring has a different base composition. Hindi ito ang first match sa thermoplastic-binder-with-mineral-filler description.
•	d. Linoleum tile/sheet – ibang classic composition ito, usually associated more with oxidized linseed oil, cork dust/wood flour, pigments, and backing.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Hanapin ang material composition clues, hindi lang name recall.
2.	May salitang thermoplastic binder at hot pressed.
3.	Tanggal si rubber at linoleum dahil ibang families sila.
4.	Between vinyl and asphalt, ang older reviewer-style description with asbestos/fibers/mineral filler points more strongly to asphalt tile/sheet.
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang vinyl tile/sheet dahil pareho silang resilient flooring.
Pero tandaan:
kapag ang stem ay old material composition type at may thermoplastic binder + asbestos/fillers, madalas ang tinutukoy ay asphalt tile.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang resilient flooring ay described as hot-pressed thermoplastic binder with fibers and mineral fillers, strongest answer is asphalt tile/sheet.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: A resilient, waterproofed floor covering that consists of a backing covered with a relatively thick layer of wearing surface processed in a special way, combined with wood, various fillers, stone dust, resin binders, driers, and inert color pigments:
keyMemory: Kapag ang floor covering ay may backing at relatively thick wear layer, strongest answer is linoleum tile/sheet.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Linoleum tile/sheet
Ulitin natin: Linoleum tile/sheet.
Ito naman, kabaligtaran ng previous item. Dito, ang clue ay hindi thermoplastic binder, kundi iyong backing with relatively thick wearing surface plus wood/fillers/driers/pigments.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang linoleum is a classic resilient floor covering with:
•	a backing
•	a built-up wear surface
•	ingredients associated with natural fillers like wood flour/cork-like filler types, pigments, binders, and driers
Iyong structure na backing + thick wearing surface is a strong clue for linoleum.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Vinyl tile/sheet – vinyl can be resilient and waterproof, yes, pero ang composition clue dito is not the strongest for vinyl.
•	b. Rubber tile/sheet – rubber flooring is quieter and resilient, but not this backed wear-surface composition description.
•	c. Asphalt tile/sheet – close trap from previous item, pero iba ang material description rito.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	May backing ba? Oo.
2.	May wearing surface ba? Oo.
3.	That layered description is very characteristic of linoleum.
4.	Huwag magpadala sa previous question at piliin ulit si asphalt. Basahin fresh ang bagong stem.
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang asphalt tile/sheet dahil galing tayo sa previous item at parehong resilient flooring.
Ito ang board-exam danger:
kapag magkasunod ang similar topics, huwag mong bitbitin ang previous answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang floor covering ay may backing at relatively thick wear layer, strongest answer is linoleum tile/sheet.

question: Parquet flooring which consists of square pieces or blocks which have been built-up in several layers like plywood and having a veneered surface, or consists of several parquet strips assembled at the factory to form a tile is also known as:
keyMemory: Parquet tile flooring = block flooring sa reviewer logic.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. block flooring
Ulitin natin: block flooring.
Note lang: may typo or duplication sa choices, kasi parehong a at b ay “block flooring.”
Pero malinaw pa rin ang intended answer.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa note mismo sa ibaba ng page, nakalagay:
“Parquet Tile Flooring – Also known as block flooring…”
So the item is directly asking for that alternate name.
Kaya ang sagot ay block flooring.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	c. floating flooring – ibang installation/system concept ito. Ito iyong floor na hindi nailed or glued directly sa subfloor in the usual sense.
•	d. plank flooring – mas malalaki at pahabang boards ito, not parquet tile/block assembly.
•	a/b. block flooring – ito ang intended correct answer kahit duplicated ang print.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Ang description ba ay square pieces or blocks assembled into a tile?
Oo.
2.	So hindi ito plank.
3.	Hindi rin ito floating kasi tanong ay tungkol sa type/name of parquet assembly, not installation method.
4.	Kaya block flooring ang natitira.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay floating flooring, lalo na kung naalala mong may engineered or layered wood products na floating installed.
Pero tandaan:
•	floating flooring = paraan ng installation/system
•	block flooring = pangalan ng parquet tile type
Important note
Kapag may ganitong reviewer item na may duplicate choices, huwag ka mataranta.
Focus ka sa stem at sa definition.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Parquet tile flooring = block flooring sa reviewer logic.

question: Is an unglazed floor tile made from natural clays or shales by the plastic method and are very durable flooring material being impervious to moisture, stains and dirt and are resistant to abrasions.
keyMemory: Kapag ang clue ay unglazed, clay/shale, durable floor tile, ang sagot ay quarry tile.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Quarry tile
Ulitin natin: Quarry tile.
Ito, ang pinakamalaking clue dito ay:
•	unglazed
•	made from natural clays or shales
•	very durable
•	impervious to moisture
•	resistant to abrasion
Iyan na iyan ang classic description ng quarry tile.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang quarry tile ay kilalang:
•	unglazed
•	matibay
•	pang-heavy use
•	bagay sa areas na kailangan durable at madaling linisin
Ito ang klase ng tile na hindi nakaasa sa glaze para tumibay. Yung tibay niya galing mismo sa body niya.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Faience Tiles – glazed decorative ceramic type ito, hindi iyong unglazed rugged floor tile na dine-describe.
•	c. Pavers – close trap ito. Matibay din at unglazed din madalas, pero mas associated ito sa paving units and larger exterior applications, hindi itong exact floor-tile description.
•	d. Ceramic Mosaic Tiles – maliliit na tiles ito, at hindi ito iyong rugged natural-clay/shale quarry-tile identity.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Unglazed ba? Oo.
2.	Natural clay/shale ba? Oo.
3.	Durable at abrasion-resistant floor tile ba? Oo.
4.	So alisin ang decorative or glazed options.
5.	Between pavers and quarry tile, ang mas exact floor-tile term ay quarry tile.
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang pavers dahil parehong clay-based at matibay.
Pero tandaan:
•	pavers = mas paving/exterior block character
•	quarry tile = classic unglazed durable floor tile
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang clue ay unglazed, clay/shale, durable floor tile, ang sagot ay quarry tile.

question: Which is not a brand of a ceramic tile?
keyMemory: Sa choices na ito, ang hindi brand ng ceramic tile ay Piedra.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Piedra
Ulitin natin: Piedra.
Ito, brand-memory item ito, pero kaya pa rin i-eliminate nang maayos.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Sa reviewer logic at sa historical Philippine ceramic-tile context, Fil-Hispano at First Lepanto are real ceramic-tile company names, at ang Euro is treated as a tile brand reference as well. Piedra ang lumalabas na outlier dito. Historical Philippine legal records also mention Fil-Hispano and First Lepanto Ceramics as ceramic-tile manufacturers. (Supreme Court E-Library)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Fil-Hispano – ceramic tile brand/company talaga ito sa Philippine context. (Supreme Court E-Library)
•	c. First Lepanto – ceramic tile manufacturer/brand reference din ito. (Supreme Court E-Library)
•	d. Euro – treated in local reviewer/market context as a tile brand reference. (FA Magazine)
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Hanapin ang choices na tunog actual ceramic companies.
2.	Fil-Hispano at First Lepanto sound like real tile manufacturers — at oo, supported sila.
3.	Euro is also a tile-brand reference in local context.
4.	Natitira ang Piedra, which sounds more like a generic Spanish word for “stone” than the intended ceramic-tile brand answer.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay Euro, kasi puwede mong isipin generic lang siyang word.
Pero sa local tile context, may Eurotiles/Euro association, kaya hindi siya ang best outlier. (FA Magazine)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa choices na ito, ang hindi brand ng ceramic tile ay Piedra.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: What is not a local brand of ceramic tiles?
keyMemory: Kapag tinanong ka ng not a local ceramic tile brand, sa choices na ito ang sagot ay Rocersa.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Rocersa
Ulitin natin: Rocersa.
Ito, mas madaling sagutin ito kung marunong kang mag-separate ng local at foreign/imported brands.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Rocersa is a Spanish ceramic-tile manufacturer, habang Eurotiles, Mariwasa, at Lepanto are treated as local Philippine tile brands/manufacturers in reviewer and market context. (Rocersa)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. Euro tiles – local Philippine brand reference. (FA Magazine)
•	c. Mariwasa – very clearly local Philippine tile manufacturer. (Mariwasa)
•	d. Lepanto – local ceramic tile manufacturer/brand reference in Philippine legal and industry context. (Supreme Court E-Library)
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	The keyword is local.
2.	Mariwasa and Lepanto are immediately strong local names.
3.	Euro tiles also belongs in the local-brand grouping in this reviewer context.
4.	So the foreign outlier is Rocersa.
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay Euro tiles, kasi puwede mong isipin imported siya dahil “Euro” ang tunog.
Pero huwag ka sa tunog lang kakapit. Sa local market/reviewer context, Eurotiles is local. (FA Magazine)
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag tinanong ka ng not a local ceramic tile brand, sa choices na ito ang sagot ay Rocersa.

question: What kind of adhesive is used in installing marble?
keyMemory: Sa reviewer logic ng page na ito, for installing marble, the best answer is tile bond.
explanation:
Best answer: d. Tile bond
Ulitin natin: Tile bond.
Aaminin ko, ito ang medyo close item, kasi sa actual practice, tile bond at tile adhesive are often used very closely in meaning. Pero sa reviewer logic ng page na ito, dito ko pipiliin ang tile bond.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
Ang tanong ay hindi definition question. Ang tanong ay:
“What kind of adhesive is used in installing marble?”
Sa site language and reviewer usage, ang practical answer diyan ay tile bond — the product/site term commonly associated with bonding tiles and stone pieces like marble.
Bakit hindi tile adhesive?
Ito ang common trap.
At reasonable trap siya, dahil technically tama rin in a general sense.
Pero tingnan mo ang next item sa page — iyon ang nagbibigay ng formal material definition. Kaya dito, ang likely intended answer is the more practical/site term: tile bond.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Grout – hindi ito pangdikit; pang-fill ng joints ito.
•	b. Plaster adhesive – maling application family ito.
•	c. Tile adhesive – close answer ito, pero the next question more strongly fits the formal definition of tile adhesive.
•	d. Tile bond – best reviewer fit for this item.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Adhesive ba ang hanap, not joint filler?
So tanggal agad si grout.
2.	Marble installation ba ito? So hindi plaster adhesive.
3.	Between tile adhesive and tile bond, ask:
“Which one sounds like the practical site answer for installing marble?”
Tile bond
4.	Then reserve the formal descriptive definition for the next item.
Common trap
Pinaka-trap talaga ang tile adhesive.
At honestly, sa actual real-world usage, malapit talaga sila.
Pero sa reviewer sequencing ng page na ito, mas logical na:
•	tile bond = practical use answer here
•	tile adhesive = formal composition answer next
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa reviewer logic ng page na ito, for installing marble, the best answer is tile bond.
visualReviewNote: Building Materials review-coach mode: question visual appears only when it is useful for visual identification; answer visuals reveal only after answering.

question: It is a polymer modified mixture of Portland cement and fine graded sand.
keyMemory: Kapag ang stem ay polymer-modified cement + fine sand adhesive mix, the formal answer is tile adhesive.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Tile adhesive
Ulitin natin: Tile adhesive.
Ito, dito na pumapasok ang formal material definition.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Current construction product references describe tile adhesive as a cement-based or polymer-modified mix of Portland cement, fine graded sand/silica sand, and additives for bonding tiles and stone. (fortressadmix.com.ph)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Tile grout – pang-joint filling ito, not the bonding mix.
•	b. Polymer grout – grout pa rin ito, so pang-fill, not adhesive.
•	d. Tile bond – close trap ito, but the formal descriptive material term in the choices is tile adhesive.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Mixture of Portland cement and fine graded sand ba? Oo.
2.	Purpose ba is bonding, not filling joints? Oo.
3.	So tanggal ang grout options.
4.	Between tile adhesive and tile bond, piliin ang formal generic material term: tile adhesive.
Common trap
Again, ang trap dito ay tile bond.
Pero dito, unlike the previous item, ang wording is very technical and descriptive, kaya mas bagay ang tile adhesive.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang stem ay polymer-modified cement + fine sand adhesive mix, the formal answer is tile adhesive.

question: A chemical agent applied on a marble floor to make it shiny without the use of wax is:
keyMemory: Marble shine without wax = crystallizer.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. crystallizer
Ulitin natin: crystallizer.
Ito, stone-floor maintenance question ito.
Bakit ito ang tama?
Ang crystallizer ang ginagamit sa marble polishing systems para:
•	mag-enhance ng shine
•	mag-react sa stone surface
•	magbigay ng glossy polished finish
•	without depending on wax
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	b. liquid tile coat – too generic/product-like, not the standard marble-polish answer
•	c. clear gloss emulsion – coating language ito, not the classic marble-shining chemical
•	d. Polyurethane – protective coating ito, but not the standard chemical used for marble polishing without wax
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Marble ba ang substrate? Oo.
2.	Shine without wax ba? Oo.
3.	So huwag sa generic coating answer pupunta.
4.	Hanapin ang stone-polishing chemical: crystallizer
Common trap
Ang trap dito ay polyurethane dahil pampakinang din naman ang dating.
Pero ibang system iyon.
Sa marble-floor maintenance, crystallizer ang classic answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Marble shine without wax = crystallizer.

question: Which of the following flooring finishes is the most slippery when exposed to dust.
keyMemory: Sa item na ito, the best answer for most slippery when exposed to dust is ceramic.
explanation:
Best answer: c. Ceramic
Ulitin natin: Ceramic.
Ito, gusto kong maging honest: in actual performance, both polished granite and terrazzo can also become slippery depending on finish and maintenance. Pero among the choices, ceramic is the strongest reviewer answer.
Bakit ito ang best answer?
A friction study comparing floor finishes found ceramic flooring to be the most slippery among the tested set, especially under low-friction conditions. Other safety references also regularly include ceramic, granite, and terrazzo among hard surfaces that become slippery, but ceramic stands out as the strongest answer here. (arpnjournals.com)
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
•	a. Granite – very slippery when polished, yes, and this is the main trap.
•	b. Carpet – obvious na hindi ito ang most slippery sa dusty condition.
•	d. Terrazzo – puwede ring madulas kapag polished, but not the strongest answer in this item.
Elimination method
Ito ang iniisip mo:
1.	Tanggal agad ang carpet.
2.	Natitira ang tatlong hard finishes.
3.	Among them, the reviewer-best and study-supported pick is ceramic.
4.	Granite is the strongest trap because polished stone also gets slippery.
Common trap
Pinaka-trap dito ang granite.
At reasonable trap siya.
Pero kapag gusto mo ng best reviewer answer, piliin mo ang ceramic.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa item na ito, the best answer for most slippery when exposed to dust is ceramic.
